Xenobiotics modify vertical energy transfer from photoautotrophic biofilms to grazers

Sophie Oster, Patrick Fink, Tobias Schmitt, Ralf Schulz, Verena C. Schreiner & Mirco Bundschuh

R script written by Sophie Oster
checked by Verena C. Schreiner


RPTU Kaiserslautern-Landau
Fortstrasse 7
76829 Landau in der Pfalz
GERMANY

This study was conducted with four chemical treatments, as explanatory variables:

  • treatment (categorical)
    • uncontaminated control (negative control, NC)
    • herbicide propyzamide 10 ug / L (Pro)
    • antibiotic ciprofloxacin 100 ug / L (Cip)
    • mixture of 10 ug / L propyzamide and 100 ug / L ciprofloxacin (Mix)

1 R version 4.3.2

R version used for this script: 4.3.2, use the following code chunk to download the correct version.

# Check the current R version
current_r_version <- as.character(getRversion())

# Specify the required R version
required_r_version <- "4.3.2"

if (current_r_version != required_r_version) {
  
  sysname <- Sys.info()[["sysname"]]
  machine <- tolower(Sys.info()[["machine"]])
  
  # Choose the correct official CRAN download
  download_url <- switch(
    sysname,
    "Windows" = sprintf(
      "https://cran.r-project.org/bin/windows/base/old/%s/R-%s-win.exe",
      required_r_version, required_r_version
    ),
    "Darwin" = if (grepl("arm64|aarch64", machine)) {
      sprintf(
        "https://cran.r-project.org/bin/macosx/big-sur-arm64/base/R-%s-arm64.pkg",
        required_r_version
      )
    } else {
      sprintf(
        "https://cran.r-project.org/bin/macosx/big-sur-x86_64/base/R-%s-x86_64.pkg",
        required_r_version
      )
    },
    "Linux" = sprintf(
      "https://cran.r-project.org/src/base/R-4/R-%s.tar.gz",
      required_r_version
    ),
    stop("Unsupported operating system: ", sysname)
  )
  
  destfile <- file.path(tempdir(), basename(download_url))
  
  message("This project requires R ", required_r_version,
          ". Downloading it from CRAN now...")
  
  download.file(download_url, destfile = destfile, mode = "wb")
  
  message("Downloaded to: ", destfile)
  
  if (sysname == "Windows") {
    shell.exec(normalizePath(destfile))
  } else if (sysname == "Darwin") {
    system2("open", shQuote(destfile), wait = FALSE)
  } else if (sysname == "Linux") {
    message(
      "Linux detected: the R source tarball was downloaded.\n",
      "Please install R ", required_r_version,
      " using your distribution's preferred method, or build from source."
    )
    utils::browseURL("https://cran.r-project.org/doc/manuals/r-patched/R-admin.html")
  }
  
  stop(
    "Please install R ", required_r_version,
    ", restart R with that version, and rerun the script."
  )
  
} else {
  message("You are already using the correct R version: ", required_r_version)
}
You are already using the correct R version: 4.3.2

2 Packages in a reproducible environment

# Restore the environment:
# renv::restore()

library(rstatix)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(tidyr)
library(broom)
library(dunn.test)
library(patchwork)
library(vegan)
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)
library(ggforce)
library(concaveman)
library(gridExtra)
library(stringr)
library(shades)
library(ggpubr)
library(ggokabeito)
library(multcomp)
library(car)
library(asbio)
library(plotrix)
library(lmtest)
library(boot)
library(moments)
library(lme4)
library(DAAG)
library(performance)
library(emmeans)
library(purrr)
library(DT)
library(Hmisc)
library(svglite)
library(mgcv)       
library(DHARMa)   
library(marginaleffects)
library(writexl)
library(ARTool)

if (!requireNamespace("devtools", quietly = TRUE)) {
  install.packages("devtools")
}

library(devtools)

if (!requireNamespace("pairwiseAdonis", quietly = TRUE)) {
  devtools::install_github("pmartinezarbizu/pairwiseAdonis/pairwiseAdonis")
}

library(pairwiseAdonis)

To examine the interaction between both xenobiotics, we further analysed their combined effects using linear models based on presence or absence of each xenobiotic. When a significant interaction was detected, we applied the post-estimation inference framework proposed by Madge Pimentel and colleagues (2025). This approach assesses interaction strength relative to additive, multiplicative, and dominance null models, and tests deviations from zero using simulation-based confidence intervals.

Load these functions:

# Madge Pimentel, I., Albini, D., Beermann, A. J., Leese, F., Macaulay, S. J., Matthaei, C. D., ... & Schäfer, R. B. (2025). Hypothesis‐Driven Research on Multiple Stressors: An Analytical Framework for Stressor Interactions. Ecology and evolution, 15(8), e71959.

# hypothesis function
hypothesis.function <- function(df.cf, stressor1, stressor2,
                                strategy = c("average", "rowwise"),
                                by = NULL,
                                null = c("ad", "mu", "do", "all")) {
  strategy <- match.arg(strategy)
  null <- match.arg(null)

  df <- df.cf |>
    dplyr::arrange(dataset, rowidcf)

  C.df <- dplyr::filter(df, dataset == "C")
  S1.df <- dplyr::filter(df, dataset == "S1")
  S2.df <- dplyr::filter(df, dataset == "S2")
  S12.df <- dplyr::filter(df, dataset == "S12")

  change_x1 <- as.numeric(S12.df[[stressor1]]) - as.numeric(C.df[[stressor1]])
  change_x2 <- as.numeric(S12.df[[stressor2]]) - as.numeric(C.df[[stressor2]])

  C <- C.df$estimate
  S1 <- S1.df$estimate
  S2 <- S2.df$estimate
  S12 <- S12.df$estimate

  if (null == "ad") {
    row.est <- (S12 - S1 - S2 + C) / (change_x1 * change_x2)
  } else if (null == "mu") {
    row.est <- (log(S12) - log(S1) - log(S2) + log(C)) / (change_x1 * change_x2)
  } else if (null == "do") {
    row.est <- (pmin(S12, C) - pmin(S1, S2)) / (change_x1 * change_x2)
  }

  if (null != "all") {
    rowwise <- C.df |>
      dplyr::select(-estimate, -dataset) |>
      dplyr::mutate(
        estimate = row.est,
        term = null,
        change_x1 = change_x1,
        change_x2 = change_x2,
        .before = 1
      )
  }

  if (null == "all") {
    row.est.ad <- (S12 - S1 - S2 + C) / (change_x1 * change_x2)
    row.est.mu <- (log(S12) - log(S1) - log(S2) + log(C)) / (change_x1 * change_x2)
    row.est.do <- (pmin(S12, C) - pmin(S1, S2)) / (change_x1 * change_x2)

    rowwise <- C.df |>
      dplyr::select(-estimate, -dataset) |>
      dplyr::mutate(
        ad = row.est.ad,
        mu = row.est.mu,
        do = row.est.do,
        change_x1 = change_x1,
        change_x2 = change_x2
      ) |>
      tidyr::pivot_longer(cols = c("ad", "mu", "do"),
                          names_to = "term", values_to = "estimate")
  }

  if (strategy == "average") {
    grouping <- "term"
    if (!is.null(by)) grouping <- c(grouping, by)
    out <- rowwise |>
      dplyr::group_by(dplyr::across(all_of(grouping))) |>
      dplyr::summarise(estimate = mean(estimate), .groups = "drop")
  } else {
    out <- rowwise
  }

  return(out)
} 

# nd generator
nd.generator <- function(df.ref, stressor1, stressor2, diff = NULL){
    df.prep <- df.ref |>
        dplyr::mutate(dataset = "C", rowidcf = dplyr::row_number())

    if (is.null(diff)) {
        df.out <- df.prep |>
            tibble::add_case(
                df.prep |> dplyr::mutate(
                    !!stressor1 := factor(levels(.data[[stressor1]])[
                        as.numeric(.data[[stressor1]]) + 1],
                        levels = levels(.data[[stressor1]])
                    ),
                    dataset = "S1"
                )
            ) |>
            tibble::add_case(
                df.prep |> dplyr::mutate(
                    !!stressor2 := factor(levels(.data[[stressor2]])[
                        as.numeric(.data[[stressor2]]) + 1],
                        levels = levels(.data[[stressor2]])
                    ),
                    dataset = "S2"
                )
            ) |>
            tibble::add_case(
                df.prep |> dplyr::mutate(
                    !!stressor1 := factor(levels(.data[[stressor1]])[
                        as.numeric(.data[[stressor1]]) + 1],
                        levels = levels(.data[[stressor1]])
                    ),
                    !!stressor2 := factor(levels(.data[[stressor2]])[
                        as.numeric(.data[[stressor2]]) + 1],
                        levels = levels(.data[[stressor2]])
                    ),
                    dataset = "S12"
                )
            )
    } else if (length(diff) == 1) {
        if (is.factor(df.prep[[stressor1]])) {
            df.out <- df.prep |>
                tibble::add_case(df.prep |> dplyr::mutate(
                    !!stressor1 := factor(levels(.data[[stressor1]])[
                        as.numeric(.data[[stressor1]]) + 1],
                        levels = levels(.data[[stressor1]])
                    ),
                    dataset = "S1"
                )) |>
                tibble::add_case(df.prep |> dplyr::mutate(
                    !!stressor2 := .data[[stressor2]] + diff,
                    dataset = "S2"
                )) |>
                tibble::add_case(df.prep |> dplyr::mutate(
                    !!stressor1 := factor(levels(.data[[stressor1]])[
                        as.numeric(.data[[stressor1]]) + 1],
                        levels = levels(.data[[stressor1]])
                    ),
                    !!stressor2 := .data[[stressor2]] + diff,
                    dataset = "S12"
                ))
        } else {
            df.out <- df.prep |>
                tibble::add_case(df.prep |> dplyr::mutate(
                    !!stressor1 := .data[[stressor1]] + diff,
                    dataset = "S1"
                )) |>
                tibble::add_case(df.prep |> dplyr::mutate(
                    !!stressor2 := factor(levels(.data[[stressor2]])[
                        as.numeric(.data[[stressor2]]) + 1],
                        levels = levels(.data[[stressor2]])
                    ),
                    dataset = "S2"
                )) |>
                tibble::add_case(df.prep |> dplyr::mutate(
                    !!stressor1 := .data[[stressor1]] + diff,
                    !!stressor2 := factor(levels(.data[[stressor2]])[
                        as.numeric(.data[[stressor2]]) + 1],
                        levels = levels(.data[[stressor2]])
                    ),
                    dataset = "S12"
                ))
        }
    } else if (length(diff) == 2) {
        df.out <- df.prep |>
            tibble::add_case(dplyr::mutate(df.prep,
                !!stressor1 := .data[[stressor1]] + diff[1],
                dataset = "S1")) |>
            tibble::add_case(dplyr::mutate(df.prep,
                !!stressor2 := .data[[stressor2]] + diff[2],
                dataset = "S2")) |>
            tibble::add_case(dplyr::mutate(df.prep,
                !!stressor1 := .data[[stressor1]] + diff[1],
                !!stressor2 := .data[[stressor2]] + diff[2],
                dataset = "S12"))
    }

    if ("rowid" %in% names(df.out)) {
        df.out <- dplyr::select(df.out, -rowid)
    }

    return(df.out)
}

3 Biofilm endpoints

Chlorophyll content and ash free dry weight (AFDW) of biofilms

  • Unit chlorophyll a, b & c: µg chlorophyll / cm²

  • Unit afdw: mg / cm²

# insert data
biofilm_endpoints <-
  read.table(file.path("data\\biofilm_endpoints.txt"),
             header = TRUE, sep = "\t")

View(biofilm_endpoints)

3.1 Chlorophyll a, b and c content

Caclulate mean, se and 95% CI chla, chlb + chlc of each treatment:

# chla
mean_chla <- biofilm_endpoints %>%
  group_by(treatment) %>%
  dplyr::summarize(
    mean_chla = mean(chla, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = sum(!is.na(chla)),
    se = sd(chla, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n),
    t_crit = qt(0.975, df = n - 1),
    ci_lower = mean_chla - t_crit * se,
    ci_upper = mean_chla + t_crit * se,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(treatment, mean_chla, ci_lower, ci_upper, se)

print(mean_chla)
# A tibble: 4 × 5
  treatment mean_chla ci_lower ci_upper     se
  <chr>         <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>  <dbl>
1 Cip           0.167   0.0873    0.248 0.0347
2 Mix           0.300   0.0585    0.542 0.105 
3 NC            0.307   0.0729    0.541 0.102 
4 Pro           0.304   0.0905    0.517 0.0925
#chlb
mean_chlb <- biofilm_endpoints %>%
  group_by(treatment) %>%
  dplyr::summarize(
    mean_chlb = mean(chlb, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = sum(!is.na(chlb)),
    se = sd(chlb, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n),
    t_crit = qt(0.975, df = n - 1),
    ci_lower = mean_chlb - t_crit * se,
    ci_upper = mean_chlb + t_crit * se,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(treatment, mean_chlb, ci_lower, ci_upper, se)

print(mean_chlb)
# A tibble: 4 × 5
  treatment mean_chlb ci_lower ci_upper      se
  <chr>         <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>   <dbl>
1 Cip          0.0513   0.0337   0.0688 0.00760
2 Mix          0.0761   0.0391   0.113  0.0160 
3 NC           0.0621   0.0217   0.102  0.0175 
4 Pro          0.0601   0.0345   0.0857 0.0111 
#chlc
mean_chlc <- biofilm_endpoints %>%
  group_by(treatment) %>%
  dplyr::summarize(
    mean_chlc = mean(chlc, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = sum(!is.na(chlc)),
    se = sd(chlc, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n),
    t_crit = qt(0.975, df = n - 1),
    ci_lower = mean_chlc - t_crit * se,
    ci_upper = mean_chlc + t_crit * se,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(treatment, mean_chlc, ci_lower, ci_upper, se)

print(mean_chlc)
# A tibble: 4 × 5
  treatment mean_chlc ci_lower ci_upper      se
  <chr>         <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>   <dbl>
1 Cip          0.0498   0.0315   0.0681 0.00793
2 Mix          0.0750   0.0463   0.104  0.0124 
3 NC           0.0563   0.0192   0.0934 0.0161 
4 Pro          0.0607   0.0403   0.0811 0.00885

3.1.1 Histogram chlorophyll a, b + c

# chl a
hist(biofilm_endpoints $ chla) 

skewness(biofilm_endpoints $ chla, na.rm = TRUE)
[1] 1.601648
shapiro.test(biofilm_endpoints $ chla)

    Shapiro-Wilk normality test

data:  biofilm_endpoints$chla
W = 0.78095, p-value = 6.966e-06
# chl b
hist(biofilm_endpoints $ chlb) 

skewness(biofilm_endpoints $ chlb, na.rm = TRUE) 
[1] 1.452618
shapiro.test(biofilm_endpoints $ chlb)

    Shapiro-Wilk normality test

data:  biofilm_endpoints$chlb
W = 0.87043, p-value = 0.0005833
# chl c
hist(biofilm_endpoints $ chlc) 

skewness(biofilm_endpoints $ chlc, na.rm = TRUE) 
[1] 1.253578
shapiro.test(biofilm_endpoints $ chlc)

    Shapiro-Wilk normality test

data:  biofilm_endpoints$chlc
W = 0.89434, p-value = 0.002398

All not normal distributed, right skewed but skewness max. 1.6 therefore no data transformation for model

3.1.2 Model chla

Linear model with xenobiotic treatment as explanatory variable.

# treatment as factor
biofilm_endpoints $ treatment <- factor(biofilm_endpoints $ treatment)

model_chla <- lm(chla ~ treatment, data = biofilm_endpoints)
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
plot(model_chla) 

qreference(residuals(model_chla)) # not normal distributed

pairwise.wilcox.test(biofilm_endpoints$chla,
                     biofilm_endpoints$treatment,
                     p.adjust.method = "holm")

    Pairwise comparisons using Wilcoxon rank sum exact test 

data:  biofilm_endpoints$chla and biofilm_endpoints$treatment 

    Cip Mix NC
Mix 1   -   - 
NC  1   1   - 
Pro 1   1   1 

P value adjustment method: holm 
# add presence / absence
# interaction effect model with presence / absence of single xenobiotics
biofilm_endpoints$Cip <- ifelse(biofilm_endpoints$treatment %in% c("Cip", "Mix"),
                                1, 0)

biofilm_endpoints$Cip <- factor(biofilm_endpoints$Cip, levels = c(0, 1))

biofilm_endpoints$Pro <- ifelse(biofilm_endpoints$treatment %in% c("Pro", "Mix"),
                                1, 0)

biofilm_endpoints$Pro <- factor(biofilm_endpoints$Pro, levels = c(0, 1))

# sanity check
table(biofilm_endpoints$treatment,
      biofilm_endpoints$Cip,
      biofilm_endpoints$Pro)
, ,  = 0

     
      0 1
  Cip 0 9
  Mix 0 0
  NC  9 0
  Pro 0 0

, ,  = 1

     
      0 1
  Cip 0 0
  Mix 0 9
  NC  0 0
  Pro 9 0
chla_mixture <- lm(chla ~ Cip * Pro, data = biofilm_endpoints)
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
plot(chla_mixture) # not normally distributed

qreference(residuals(chla_mixture)) 

kruskal_chla_interaction <- biofilm_endpoints %>%
  dplyr::mutate(treatment = interaction(Cip, Pro, sep = "_")) %>%
  kruskal.test(chla ~ treatment, data = .)

kruskal_chla_interaction

    Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test

data:  chla by treatment
Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 1.3063, df = 3, p-value = 0.7276
# no significant interaction

No significant differences between chemical treatments in chlorophyll a content.

3.1.3 Model chlb

model_chlb <- lm(chlb ~ treatment, data = biofilm_endpoints)
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
plot(model_chlb)

qreference(residuals(model_chlb)) # normality acceptable

drop1(model_chlb, test = 'F')
Single term deletions

Model:
chlb ~ treatment
          Df Sum of Sq      RSS     AIC F value Pr(>F)
<none>                 0.053639 -226.32               
treatment  3 0.0028604 0.056500 -230.45  0.5688 0.6396
chlb_inter <- lsmeans(model_chlb, ~ treatment)
chlb_inter_dunn1 <- contrast(chlb_inter, 'pairwise')
(chlb_inter_dunn_sum1 <- summary(chlb_inter_dunn1, infer = TRUE, adjust = 'mvt'))
 contrast  estimate     SE df lower.CL upper.CL t.ratio p.value
 Cip - Mix -0.02487 0.0193 32  -0.0771   0.0274  -1.289  0.5767
 Cip - NC  -0.01080 0.0193 32  -0.0631   0.0415  -0.560  0.9432
 Cip - Pro -0.00888 0.0193 32  -0.0612   0.0434  -0.460  0.9672
 Mix - NC   0.01407 0.0193 32  -0.0382   0.0663   0.729  0.8847
 Mix - Pro  0.01599 0.0193 32  -0.0363   0.0683   0.829  0.8405
 NC - Pro   0.00192 0.0193 32  -0.0504   0.0542   0.100  0.9996

Confidence level used: 0.95 
Conf-level adjustment: mvt method for 6 estimates 
P value adjustment: mvt method for 6 tests

No significant differences between chemical treatments in chlorophyll b content. Still assess potential interaction:

chlb_mixture <- lm(chlb ~ Cip * Pro, data = biofilm_endpoints)
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
plot(chlb_mixture) # normality acceptable

qreference(residuals(chlb_mixture)) 

drop1(chlb_mixture, test = 'F')
Single term deletions

Model:
chlb ~ Cip * Pro
        Df Sum of Sq      RSS     AIC F value Pr(>F)
<none>               0.053639 -226.32               
Cip:Pro  1 0.0016149 0.055254 -227.26  0.9634 0.3337
# no significant interaction

3.1.4 Model chlc

model_chlc <- lm(chlc ~ treatment, data = biofilm_endpoints)
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
plot(model_chlc) # normality acceptable

qreference(residuals(model_chlc))

drop1(model_chlc, test = 'F')
Single term deletions

Model:
chlc ~ treatment
          Df Sum of Sq      RSS     AIC F value Pr(>F)
<none>                 0.039915 -236.96               
treatment  3 0.0030658 0.042981 -240.30  0.8193 0.4928

No significant differences between chemical treatments in chlorophyll c content. Still check for interactions of Cip * Pro:

chlc_mixture <- lm(chlc ~ Cip * Pro, data = biofilm_endpoints)
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
plot(chlc_mixture) # normality acceptable

qreference(residuals(chlc_mixture)) 

drop1(chlc_mixture, test = 'F')
Single term deletions

Model:
chlc ~ Cip * Pro
        Df Sum of Sq      RSS     AIC F value Pr(>F)
<none>               0.039915 -236.96               
Cip:Pro  1 0.0009716 0.040887 -238.10  0.7789 0.3841
# no significant interaction

3.1.5 Graphs chlorophyll a, b + c

# chl a
chla_plot <-
  ggplot(data = biofilm_endpoints,
         mapping = aes(x = factor(
           treatment, levels = c("NC", "Pro", "Cip", "Mix")
         ), y = chla)) +
  # Mean
  stat_summary(
    fun = mean,
    geom = "point",
    aes(fill = treatment),
    shape = 21,
    size = 5,
    color = "grey20",
    stroke = 0.5
  ) +
  
  # 95% CI
  # Black outline (thicker, underneath)
  stat_summary(
    fun.data = mean_cl_normal,
    geom = "errorbar",
    aes(group = treatment),
    width = 0.2,
    linewidth = 1,
    color = "grey30"
  ) +
  
  # Colored CI on top
  stat_summary(
    fun.data = mean_cl_normal,
    geom = "errorbar",
    aes(color = treatment),
    width = 0.2,
    linewidth = 0.8
  ) +
  
  # Jittered
  geom_point(
    aes(color = treatment),
    position = position_jitter(width = 0.25, height = 0),
    alpha = 0.8,
    size = 2
  ) +
  
  # Color scales
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "NC" = "#009E73",
    "Pro" = "#E69F00",
    "Cip" = "#56B4E9",
    "Mix" = "#999999"
  )) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "NC" = "#009E73",
    "Pro" = "#E69F00",
    "Cip" = "#56B4E9",
    "Mix" = "#999999"
  )) +
  guides(fill = "none") +
  
  # Theme and labels
  labs(x = NULL,
       y = expression(bold("Chl a (µg/cm" ^ "2" * ")")),
       color = "") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "grey100"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(
      size = 13,
      face = "bold",
      colour = "grey30"
  ),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12),  
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
    panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA, colour = "grey30"),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(colour = "grey85", size = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(colour = "grey95", size = 0.2)
  ) 
Warning: The `size` argument of `element_line()` is deprecated as of ggplot2 3.4.0.
ℹ Please use the `linewidth` argument instead.
# Chlorophyll b
chlb_plot <-
  ggplot(data = biofilm_endpoints,
         mapping = aes(x = factor(
           treatment, levels = c("NC", "Pro", "Cip", "Mix")
         ), y = chlb)) +
  # Mean
  stat_summary(
    fun = mean,
    geom = "point",
    aes(fill = treatment),
    shape = 21,
    size = 5,
    color = "grey20",
    stroke = 0.5
  ) +
  
  # 95% CI
  # Black outline (thicker, underneath)
  stat_summary(
    fun.data = mean_cl_normal,
    geom = "errorbar",
    aes(group = treatment),
    width = 0.2,
    linewidth = 1,
    color = "grey30"
  ) +
  
  # Colored CI on top
  stat_summary(
    fun.data = mean_cl_normal,
    geom = "errorbar",
    aes(color = treatment),
    width = 0.2,
    linewidth = 0.8
  ) +
  
  # Jittered
  geom_point(
    aes(color = treatment),
    position = position_jitter(width = 0.25, height = 0),
    alpha = 0.8,
    size = 2
  ) +
  
  # Color scales
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "NC" = "#009E73",
    "Pro" = "#E69F00",
    "Cip" = "#56B4E9",
    "Mix" = "#999999"
  )) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "NC" = "#009E73",
    "Pro" = "#E69F00",
    "Cip" = "#56B4E9",
    "Mix" = "#999999"
  )) +
  guides(fill = "none") +
  
  # Theme and labels
  labs(x = NULL,
       y = expression(bold("Chl b (µg/cm" ^ "2" * ")")),
       color = "") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "grey100"),
    axis.title = element_text(
      size = 13,
      face = "bold",
      colour = "grey30"
    ),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12),  
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
    panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA, colour = "grey30"),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(colour = "grey85", size = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(colour = "grey95", size = 0.2)
  ) 

# Chlorophyll c
chlc_plot <-
  ggplot(data = biofilm_endpoints,
         mapping = aes(x = factor(
           treatment, levels = c("NC", "Pro", "Cip", "Mix")
         ), y = chlc)) +
  # Mean
  stat_summary(
    fun = mean,
    geom = "point",
    aes(fill = treatment),
    shape = 21,
    size = 5,
    color = "grey20",
    stroke = 0.5
  ) +
  
  # 95% CI
  # Black outline (thicker, underneath)
  stat_summary(
    fun.data = mean_cl_normal,
    geom = "errorbar",
    aes(group = treatment),
    width = 0.2,
    linewidth = 1,
    color = "grey30"
  ) +
  
  # Colored CI on top
  stat_summary(
    fun.data = mean_cl_normal,
    geom = "errorbar",
    aes(color = treatment),
    width = 0.2,
    linewidth = 0.8
  ) +
  
  # Jittered
  geom_point(
    aes(color = treatment),
    position = position_jitter(width = 0.25, height = 0),
    alpha = 0.8,
    size = 2
  ) +
  
  # Color scales
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "NC" = "#009E73",
    "Pro" = "#E69F00",
    "Cip" = "#56B4E9",
    "Mix" = "#999999"
  )) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "NC" = "#009E73",
    "Pro" = "#E69F00",
    "Cip" = "#56B4E9",
    "Mix" = "#999999"
  )) +
  guides(fill = "none") +
  
  # Theme and labels
  labs(x = "Treatment",
       y = expression(bold("Chl c (µg/cm" ^ "2" * ")")),
       color = "") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Control", "Propyzamide", "Ciprofloxacin", "Mixture")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "grey100"),
    axis.title = element_text(
      size = 13,
      face = "bold",
      colour = "grey30"
    ),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 11),  
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
    panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA, colour = "grey30"),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(colour = "grey85", size = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(colour = "grey95", size = 0.2)
  ) 

# combine and save with AFDW plot later

3.2 AFDW

Calculate mean, se and 95% CI AFDW of each treatment.

mean_afdw <- biofilm_endpoints %>%
  group_by(treatment) %>%
  dplyr::summarize(
    mean_afdw = mean(afdw, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = sum(!is.na(afdw)),
    se = sd(afdw, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n),
    t_crit = qt(0.975, df = n - 1),
    ci_lower = mean_afdw - t_crit * se,
    ci_upper = mean_afdw + t_crit * se,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(treatment, mean_afdw, ci_lower, ci_upper, se)

print(mean_afdw)
# A tibble: 4 × 5
  treatment mean_afdw ci_lower ci_upper     se
  <fct>         <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>  <dbl>
1 Cip           0.125   0.0832    0.167 0.0182
2 Mix           0.142   0.0430    0.240 0.0428
3 NC            0.273   0.104     0.441 0.0730
4 Pro           0.226   0.0749    0.378 0.0657

3.2.1 Histogram AFDW after colonisation

hist(biofilm_endpoints $ afdw) 

skewness(biofilm_endpoints $ afdw, na.rm = TRUE) 
[1] 1.433642
shapiro.test(biofilm_endpoints $ afdw)

    Shapiro-Wilk normality test

data:  biofilm_endpoints$afdw
W = 0.759, p-value = 2.751e-06

Not normal distributed, right skewed but skewness <1.5 therefore no data transformation for model.

3.2.2 Model AFDW

model_afdw <- lm(afdw ~ treatment, data = biofilm_endpoints)
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
plot(model_afdw) # rather s shape

qreference(residuals(model_afdw))

pairwise.wilcox.test(biofilm_endpoints$afdw,
                     biofilm_endpoints$treatment,
                     p.adjust.method = "holm")

    Pairwise comparisons using Wilcoxon rank sum exact test 

data:  biofilm_endpoints$afdw and biofilm_endpoints$treatment 

    Cip  Mix  NC  
Mix 1.00 -    -   
NC  0.68 0.51 -   
Pro 1.00 1.00 1.00

P value adjustment method: holm

No significant differences for treatment variable of total biofilm biomass.

3.2.3 Boxplot AFDW

afdw_boxplot <-
  ggplot(data = biofilm_endpoints,
         mapping = aes(x = factor(
           treatment, levels = c("NC", "Pro", "Cip", "Mix")
         ), y = afdw)) +
  # Mean
  stat_summary(
    fun = mean,
    geom = "point",
    aes(fill = treatment),
    shape = 21,
    size = 5,
    color = "grey20",
    stroke = 0.5
  ) +
  
  # 95% CI
  # Black outline (thicker, underneath)
  stat_summary(
    fun.data = mean_cl_normal,
    geom = "errorbar",
    aes(group = treatment),
    width = 0.2,
    linewidth = 1,
    color = "grey30"
  ) +
  
  # Colored CI on top
  stat_summary(
    fun.data = mean_cl_normal,
    geom = "errorbar",
    aes(color = treatment),
    width = 0.2,
    linewidth = 0.8
  ) +
  
  # Jittered
  geom_point(
    aes(color = treatment),
    position = position_jitter(width = 0.25, height = 0),
    alpha = 0.8,
    size = 2
  ) +
  
  # Color scales
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "NC" = "#009E73",
    "Pro" = "#E69F00",
    "Cip" = "#56B4E9",
    "Mix" = "#999999"
  )) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "NC" = "#009E73",
    "Pro" = "#E69F00",
    "Cip" = "#56B4E9",
    "Mix" = "#999999"
  )) +
  guides(fill = "none") +
  
  # Theme and labels
  labs(x = "Treatment",
       y = expression(bold("AFDW (mg/cm"^"2"*")")),
       color = "") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Control", "Propyzamide", "Ciprofloxacin", "Mixture")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "grey100"),
    axis.title = element_text(
      size = 13,
      face = "bold",
      colour = "grey30"
    ),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 11),  
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
    panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA, colour = "grey30"),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(colour = "grey85", size = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(colour = "grey95", size = 0.2)
  ) 


afdw_boxplot 

# Now combine with chlorophyll plots
combined_plot <- chla_plot / chlb_plot / chlc_plot / afdw_boxplot + 
  plot_layout(ncol = 2) 

# save the plot
ggsave(
  "output/biofilm_endpoints.png",
  plot = combined_plot,
  width = 25,
  height = 30,
  units = "cm",
  dpi = 600
)

4 Fatty acid composition biofilm

Statistics for fatty acid composition of the biofilm in absolute values (µg fatty acid / mg dry weight biofilm).

# insert data
FA_Biofilm_absolute <-
  read.table(file.path("data\\FA_Biofilm_absolute.txt"),
             header = TRUE,
             sep = "\t")

View(FA_Biofilm_absolute)

4.1 Absolute SAFA, MUFA & PUFA concentrations

1) Total SAFA content

# total SAFA, MUFA, PUFA content; ba = "biofilm absolute"
# total SAFA content
# filter saturated fatty acids in new table
SAFA_ba <- FA_Biofilm_absolute %>%
    filter(fa %in% c("C08:0", "C10:0", "C11:0","C12:0", "C13:0", "C14:0", "C15:0", "C16:0", "C18:0", "C20:0", "C21:0", "C22:0", "C24:0"))

# Sum SAFA per replicate and treatment
total_SAFA_per_replicate <- SAFA_ba %>%
  dplyr::group_by(replicate, treatment) %>%
  dplyr::summarize(sum_SAFA = sum(fa_conc), .groups = "drop")
print(total_SAFA_per_replicate)
# A tibble: 12 × 3
   replicate treatment sum_SAFA
   <chr>     <chr>        <dbl>
 1 B_1       NC            84.3
 2 B_10      Cip           67.8
 3 B_11      Pro           68.0
 4 B_12      Mix           64.4
 5 B_2       Cip           72.1
 6 B_3       Pro           36.5
 7 B_4       Mix           90.4
 8 B_5       NC            83.0
 9 B_6       Cip           81.2
10 B_7       Pro           71.3
11 B_8       Mix           61.1
12 B_9       NC           122. 
# Statistical differences
hist(total_SAFA_per_replicate $ sum_SAFA) # normal distributed

shapiro.test(total_SAFA_per_replicate $ sum_SAFA) # normal distributed

    Shapiro-Wilk normality test

data:  total_SAFA_per_replicate$sum_SAFA
W = 0.92447, p-value = 0.3252
leveneTest(sum_SAFA ~ treatment, data = total_SAFA_per_replicate) # homogenous
Levene's Test for Homogeneity of Variance (center = median)
      Df F value Pr(>F)
group  3  0.1736 0.9112
       8               
anova_SAFA <- aov(sum_SAFA ~ treatment, data = total_SAFA_per_replicate)
summary(anova_SAFA)
            Df Sum Sq Mean Sq F value Pr(>F)
treatment    3   2241   746.9   2.543   0.13
Residuals    8   2350   293.8               
TukeyHSD(anova_SAFA)
  Tukey multiple comparisons of means
    95% family-wise confidence level

Fit: aov(formula = sum_SAFA ~ treatment, data = total_SAFA_per_replicate)

$treatment
              diff       lwr       upr     p adj
Mix-Cip  -1.723539 -46.53926 43.092185 0.9992740
NC-Cip   22.927247 -21.88848 67.742971 0.4115762
Pro-Cip -15.088489 -59.90421 29.727236 0.7114942
NC-Mix   24.650786 -20.16494 69.466510 0.3559698
Pro-Mix -13.364950 -58.18067 31.450774 0.7774737
Pro-NC  -38.015736 -82.83146  6.799988 0.0992573
# Calculate mean, se and 95% CI per treatment
mean_SAFA_ba <- total_SAFA_per_replicate %>%
  dplyr::group_by(treatment) %>%
  dplyr::summarize(
    mean_SAFA = mean(sum_SAFA, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = sum(!is.na(sum_SAFA)),
    se = sd(sum_SAFA, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n),
    t_crit = qt(0.975, df = n - 1),
    ci_lower = mean_SAFA - t_crit * se,
    ci_upper = mean_SAFA + t_crit * se,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(treatment, mean_SAFA, ci_lower, ci_upper, se)

# Extract mean of control group for percental difference
control_mean_SAFA_ba <- mean_SAFA_ba %>%
  filter(treatment == "NC") %>%
  pull(mean_SAFA)

# Calculate percental difference from control
mean_SAFA_ba <- mean_SAFA_ba %>%
  mutate(percent_diff_from_NC = 100 * (mean_SAFA - control_mean_SAFA_ba) / control_mean_SAFA_ba)

print(mean_SAFA_ba)
# A tibble: 4 × 6
  treatment mean_SAFA ci_lower ci_upper    se percent_diff_from_NC
  <chr>         <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> <dbl>                <dbl>
1 Cip            73.7     56.7     90.6  3.94                -23.7
2 Mix            72.0     32.1    112.   9.28                -25.5
3 NC             96.6     40.9    152.  12.9                   0  
4 Pro            58.6     10.9    106.  11.1                 -39.3

2) Total MUFA content

# filter mono-unsaturated fatty acids in new table
MUFA_ba <- FA_Biofilm_absolute %>%
  filter(fa %in% c("C14:1", "C15:1","C16:1", "C17:1", "C18:1", "C20:1","C22:1", "C24:1"))

# Sum MUFA per replicate and treatment
total_MUFA_per_replicate <- MUFA_ba %>%
  dplyr::group_by(replicate, treatment) %>%
  dplyr::summarize(sum_MUFA = sum(fa_conc), .groups = "drop")
print(total_MUFA_per_replicate)

11 B_8       Mix           58.6
12 B_9       NC            97.3
# Statistical differences

    Shapiro-Wilk normality test

# Calculate mean, se and 95% CI per treatment
mean_MUFA_ba <- total_MUFA_per_replicate %>%
  dplyr::group_by(treatment) %>%
  dplyr::summarize(
    mean_MUFA = mean(sum_MUFA, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = sum(!is.na(sum_MUFA)),
    se = sd(sum_MUFA, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n),
    t_crit = qt(0.975, df = n - 1),
    ci_lower = mean_MUFA - t_crit * se,
    ci_upper = mean_MUFA + t_crit * se,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(treatment, mean_MUFA, ci_lower, ci_upper, se)

# Extract mean of control group for percental difference
control_mean_MUFA_ba <- mean_MUFA_ba %>%
  filter(treatment == "NC") %>%
  pull(mean_MUFA)

# Calculate percental difference from control
mean_MUFA_ba <- mean_MUFA_ba %>%
  mutate(percent_diff_from_NC = 100 * (mean_MUFA - control_mean_MUFA_ba) / control_mean_MUFA_ba)

print(mean_MUFA_ba)

  treatment mean_MUFA ci_lower ci_upper    se percent_diff_from_NC

3) Total PUFA content

# filter poly-unsaturated fatty acids in new table
PUFA_ba <- FA_Biofilm_absolute %>%
  filter(fa %in% c("C18:2(n-6c)", "C18:2(n-6t)", "C18:3(n-3)", "C18:3(n-6)", "C18:4(n-3)", "C20:2(n-6)", "C20:3(n-3)", "C20:3(n-6)", "C20:4(n-6)", "C20:5(n-3)", "C22:2", "C22:5(n-3)", "C22:6(n-3)"))
  
# Sum PUFA per replicate and treatment
total_PUFA_per_replicate <- PUFA_ba %>%
  dplyr::group_by(replicate, treatment) %>%
  dplyr::summarize(sum_PUFA = sum(fa_conc), .groups = "drop")
print(total_PUFA_per_replicate)

# Calculate mean, se and 95% CI per treatment
mean_PUFA_ba <- total_PUFA_per_replicate %>%
  dplyr::group_by(treatment) %>%
  dplyr::summarize(
    mean_PUFA = mean(sum_PUFA, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = sum(!is.na(sum_PUFA)),
    se = sd(sum_PUFA, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n),
    t_crit = qt(0.975, df = n - 1),
    ci_lower = mean_PUFA - t_crit * se,
    ci_upper = mean_PUFA + t_crit * se,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(treatment, mean_PUFA, ci_lower, ci_upper, se)

# Extract mean of control group for percental difference
control_mean_PUFA_ba <- mean_PUFA_ba %>%
  filter(treatment == "NC") %>%
  pull(mean_PUFA)

# Calculate percental difference from control
mean_PUFA_ba <- mean_PUFA_ba %>%
  mutate(percent_diff_from_NC = 100 * (mean_PUFA - control_mean_PUFA_ba) / control_mean_PUFA_ba)

print(mean_PUFA_ba)
# A tibble: 4 × 6
  treatment mean_PUFA ci_lower ci_upper    se percent_diff_from_NC
  <chr>         <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> <dbl>                <dbl>
1 Cip            94.1     32.7    155.  14.3                 -12.3
2 Mix            78.8    -10.6    168.  20.8                 -26.6
3 NC            107.      31.5    183.  17.6                   0  
4 Pro            73.3     48.1     98.5  5.86                -31.7

SAFA, MUFA & PUFA plot relative to control:

prep_class_from_replicates <- function(df_rep, value_col, class_name){
  df_rep %>%
    group_by(treatment) %>%
    summarise(
      mean = mean(.data[[value_col]], na.rm = TRUE),
      n    = sum(!is.na(.data[[value_col]])),
      se   = sd(.data[[value_col]], na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n),
      tcrit = qt(0.975, df = n - 1),
      lwr  = mean - tcrit * se,
      upr  = mean + tcrit * se,
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(Class = class_name) %>%
    dplyr::select(treatment, Class, mean, lwr, upr)
}

biofilm_class_abs <- bind_rows(
  prep_class_from_replicates(total_SAFA_per_replicate, "sum_SAFA", "SAFA"),
  prep_class_from_replicates(total_MUFA_per_replicate, "sum_MUFA", "MUFA"),
  prep_class_from_replicates(total_PUFA_per_replicate, "sum_PUFA", "PUFA")
) %>%
  mutate(
    Class = factor(Class, levels = c("SAFA","PUFA","MUFA")),
    treatment = factor(treatment, levels = c("NC", "Cip","Pro","Mix"))
  )

biofilm_class_abs
# A tibble: 12 × 5
   treatment Class  mean   lwr   upr
   <fct>     <fct> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>
 1 Cip       SAFA   73.7  56.7  90.6
 2 Mix       SAFA   72.0  32.1 112. 
 3 NC        SAFA   96.6  40.9 152. 
 4 Pro       SAFA   58.6  10.9 106. 
 5 Cip       MUFA   82.1  47.7 116. 
 6 Mix       MUFA   47.3  16.1  78.6
 7 NC        MUFA   86.2  27.4 145. 
 8 Pro       MUFA   65.2  43.9  86.5
 9 Cip       PUFA   94.1  32.7 155. 
10 Mix       PUFA   78.8 -10.6 168. 
11 NC        PUFA  107.   31.5 183. 
12 Pro       PUFA   73.3  48.1  98.5
# relative values
biofilm_class_rel <- biofilm_class_abs %>%
  group_by(Class) %>%
  mutate(
    ctrl_mean = mean[treatment == "NC"][1],
    mean = mean / ctrl_mean,
    lwr  = lwr  / ctrl_mean,
    upr  = upr  / ctrl_mean
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  dplyr::select(-ctrl_mean)


plot_df <- biofilm_class_rel  

col_xeno <- c(
  "Pro" = "#E69F00",
  "Cip" = "#56B4E9",
  "Mix" = "#999999"
)

plot_df <- plot_df %>%
  mutate(
    show = ifelse(treatment == "NC", 0, 1),  
    treatment = factor(treatment, levels = c("NC","Pro","Cip","Mix")),
    Class = factor(Class, levels = c("PUFA", "MUFA", "SAFA")),
    lwr = ifelse(lwr < 0, 0, lwr)
  )

# plot
fa_biofilm_relative_changes <- ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = mean, y = Class)) +

  geom_vline(xintercept = 1, linewidth = 1, colour = "grey30") +

  geom_errorbarh(
    aes(xmin = lwr, xmax = upr, group = treatment, alpha = show),
    height = 0,
    linewidth = 1,
    colour = "grey30",
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.45),
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +

  geom_errorbarh(
    aes(xmin = lwr, xmax = upr, colour = treatment, alpha = show),
    height = 0,
    linewidth = 0.8,
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.45),
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +

  geom_point(
    aes(colour = treatment, alpha = show),
    size = 4,
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.45),
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +

  scale_colour_manual(
    values = c(
    "NC" = "#009E73",
    "Pro" = "#E69F00",
    "Cip" = "#56B4E9",
    "Mix" = "#999999"
    ),
    labels = c(
      "NC"  = "Control",
      "Cip" = "Ciprofloxacin",
      "Pro" = "Propyzamide",
      "Mix" = "Mixture"
    ),
    name = "Treatment"
  ) +

  scale_alpha_identity() +

  guides(
    colour = guide_legend(override.aes = list(alpha = 1))
  ) +

  labs(
    x = "Fold change relative to control",
    y = NULL
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold", colour = "grey30"),
    legend.text  = element_text(size = 11, colour = "grey30"),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "grey100"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold", colour = "grey30"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold", colour = "grey30"),
    panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA, colour = "grey30"),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(colour = "grey95", size = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(colour = "grey95", size = 0.2)
  )

fa_biofilm_relative_changes

# save the plot
ggsave(
  "output/fa_biofilm_relative_changes.png",
  plot = fa_biofilm_relative_changes,
  width = 16.5,
  height = 12,
  units = "cm",
  dpi = 600
)

4) Total FA content

# total FA 
total_FA_biofilm <- bind_rows(total_SAFA_per_replicate, total_MUFA_per_replicate, total_PUFA_per_replicate)

total_FA_per_replicate <- total_FA_biofilm %>%
  dplyr::group_by(replicate, treatment) %>%
  dplyr::summarize(sum_FA = sum(sum_SAFA, sum_MUFA, sum_PUFA, na.rm = TRUE))
`summarise()` has grouped output by 'replicate'. You can override using the
`.groups` argument.
print(total_FA_per_replicate)
# A tibble: 12 × 3
# Groups:   replicate [12]
   replicate treatment sum_FA
   <chr>     <chr>      <dbl>
 1 B_1       NC          260.
 2 B_10      Cip         258.
 3 B_11      Pro         192.
 4 B_12      Mix         216.
 5 B_2       Cip         227.
 6 B_3       Pro         189.
 7 B_4       Mix         213.
 8 B_5       NC          259.
 9 B_6       Cip         264.
10 B_7       Pro         210.
11 B_8       Mix         166.
12 B_9       NC          352.
# statistical differences will be calculated by PERMANOVA

# Calculate mean, se and 95% CI per treatment
mean_FA_ba <- total_FA_per_replicate %>%
  dplyr::group_by(treatment) %>%
  dplyr::summarize(
    mean_FA = mean(sum_FA, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = sum(!is.na(sum_FA)),
    se = sd(sum_FA, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n),
    t_crit = qt(0.975, df = n - 1),
    ci_lower = mean_FA - t_crit * se,
    ci_upper = mean_FA + t_crit * se,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(treatment, mean_FA, ci_lower, ci_upper, se)

# Extract mean of control group for percental difference
control_mean_FA_ba <- mean_FA_ba %>%
  filter(treatment == "NC") %>%
  pull(mean_FA)

# Calculate percental difference from control
mean_FA_ba <- mean_FA_ba %>%
  mutate(percent_diff_from_NC = 100 * (mean_FA - control_mean_FA_ba) / control_mean_FA_ba)

print(mean_FA_ba)
# A tibble: 4 × 6
  treatment mean_FA ci_lower ci_upper    se percent_diff_from_NC
  <chr>       <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> <dbl>                <dbl>
1 Cip          250.     201.     299. 11.3                 -13.9
2 Mix          198.     129.     268. 16.1                 -31.7
3 NC           290.     158.     423. 30.8                   0  
4 Pro          197.     169.     226.  6.63                -32.1

4.2 Histogram FA biofilm

hist(FA_Biofilm_absolute $ fa_conc) 

skewness(FA_Biofilm_absolute $ fa_conc, na.rm = TRUE) 
[1] 2.723041
shapiro.test(FA_Biofilm_absolute $ fa_conc)

    Shapiro-Wilk normality test

data:  FA_Biofilm_absolute$fa_conc
W = 0.56951, p-value < 2.2e-16

Biofilm fatty acid concentration not normal distributed and right skewed.

4.3 NMDS FA biofilm

Change table to wide format:

# change table to wide format
FA_Biofilm_absolute_wide_sqrt <-
  pivot_wider(FA_Biofilm_absolute,
              names_from = fa,
              values_from = fa_conc)

# square root transformation, to reduce discriminatory power of dominant fa groups
FA_Biofilm_absolute_wide_sqrt[, 4:37] <- sqrt(FA_Biofilm_absolute_wide_sqrt[, 4:37])

View(FA_Biofilm_absolute_wide_sqrt)

# Only keep columns/FAs with more than 5 non-zero values (fatty acids that have just been found in 5 replicates will be removed)
FA_Biofilm_absolute_wide_sqrt <-
  FA_Biofilm_absolute_wide_sqrt %>%
  dplyr::select(
    1:3,
    where(~sum(. !=0, na.rm = TRUE)>5)
  )

Perform metaMDS:

# Rename and ease col-names for later NMDS plot
FA_Biofilm_absolute_wide_sqrt <- FA_Biofilm_absolute_wide_sqrt %>%
  rename(
    `12:0`     = `C12:0`,   
    `13:0`     = `C13:0`,
    `14:0`     = `C14:0`,
    `15:0`     = `C15:0`,
    `16:0`     = `C16:0`,
    `16:1`     = `C16:1`,
    `17:1`     = `C17:1`,
    `18:0`     = `C18:0`,
    `18:1`     = `C18:1`,
    `18:2n-6c` = `C18:2(n-6c)`,
    `18:3n-6`  = `C18:3(n-6)`,
    `18:3n-3`  = `C18:3(n-3)`,
    `18:4n-3`  = `C18:4(n-3)`,
    `20:0`     = `C20:0`,
    `20:1`     = `C20:1`,
    `20:2n-6`  = `C20:2(n-6)`,
    `20:4n-6`  = `C20:4(n-6)`,
    `20:5n-3`  = `C20:5(n-3)`,
    `22:0`     = `C22:0`,
    `22:1`     = `C22:1`,
    `22:6n-3`  = `C22:6(n-3)`,
    `24:0`     = `C24:0`
  )

# Remove columns 1:3 for metaMDS
FA_Biofilm_absolute_wide_sqrt <- FA_Biofilm_absolute_wide_sqrt[, -c(1, 2, 3)]

NMDS_Biofilm_absolute <-
  metaMDS(
    FA_Biofilm_absolute_wide_sqrt,
    autotransform = FALSE,
    k = 2 ,
    distance = "bray",
    na.rm = TRUE
  )
Run 0 stress 0.09760298 
Run 1 stress 0.09708284 
... New best solution
... Procrustes: rmse 0.1400175  max resid 0.3858864 
Run 2 stress 0.1075762 
Run 3 stress 0.1202608 
Run 4 stress 0.3164992 
Run 5 stress 0.09708267 
... New best solution
... Procrustes: rmse 0.0001755346  max resid 0.0003649831 
... Similar to previous best
Run 6 stress 0.1054514 
Run 7 stress 0.1075758 
Run 8 stress 0.1054514 
Run 9 stress 0.1054514 
Run 10 stress 0.1226817 
Run 11 stress 0.1118735 
Run 12 stress 0.09708261 
... New best solution
... Procrustes: rmse 0.0002078145  max resid 0.0004376907 
... Similar to previous best
Run 13 stress 0.09708264 
... Procrustes: rmse 8.506415e-05  max resid 0.0001810253 
... Similar to previous best
Run 14 stress 0.09760297 
Run 15 stress 0.120582 
Run 16 stress 0.09760296 
Run 17 stress 0.09708273 
... Procrustes: rmse 0.0002696143  max resid 0.0005682666 
... Similar to previous best
Run 18 stress 0.1226817 
Run 19 stress 0.1202608 
Run 20 stress 0.09708263 
... Procrustes: rmse 0.0001233286  max resid 0.000260299 
... Similar to previous best
*** Best solution repeated 4 times
NMDS_Biofilm_absolute$stress
[1] 0.09708261

Create fa vectors:

# separation in SAFA; MUFA & PUFA to build vectors in NMDS
# "ba" = biofilm absolute
FA_variables.SAFAs_ba <-
  envfit(
    NMDS_Biofilm_absolute,
    subset(
      FA_Biofilm_absolute_wide_sqrt,
      select = c(
        "12:0",
        "13:0",
        "14:0",
        "15:0",
        "16:0",
        "18:0",
        "20:0",
        "22:0",
        "24:0"
      )
    )
    ,
    permutations = 999,
    na.rm = TRUE
  )

FA_variables.MUFAs_ba <-
  envfit(
    NMDS_Biofilm_absolute,
    subset(
      FA_Biofilm_absolute_wide_sqrt,
      select = c(
        "16:1",
        "17:1",
        "18:1",
        "20:1",
        "22:1"
      )
    )
    ,
    permutations = 999,
    na.rm = TRUE
  )

FA_variables.PUFAs_ba <-
  envfit(
    NMDS_Biofilm_absolute,
    subset(
      FA_Biofilm_absolute_wide_sqrt,
      select = c(
        "18:2n-6c",
        "18:3n-6",
        "18:3n-3",
        "18:4n-3",
        "20:2n-6",
        "20:4n-6",
        "20:5n-3",
        "22:6n-3"
      )
    )
    ,
    permutations = 999,
    na.rm = TRUE
  )

# Extract NMDS scores
data.scores_biofilm_absolute = as.data.frame(scores(NMDS_Biofilm_absolute)$sites)

Treatments <-
  FA_Biofilm_absolute$treatment[FA_Biofilm_absolute$fa == "C16:0"]

# add treatment column to NMDS scores
data.scores_biofilm_absolute$Treatment = Treatments

# vectors for SAFA, MUFA and PUFA
SAFAs_coord_cont_ba = as.data.frame(scores(FA_variables.SAFAs_ba, "vectors")) * ordiArrowMul(FA_variables.SAFAs_ba)
MUFAs_coord_cont_ba = as.data.frame(scores(FA_variables.MUFAs_ba, "vectors")) * ordiArrowMul(FA_variables.MUFAs_ba)
PUFAs_coord_cont_ba = as.data.frame(scores(FA_variables.PUFAs_ba, "vectors")) * ordiArrowMul(FA_variables.PUFAs_ba)

Plot NMDS:

# Reorder treatment variable for the plot
data.scores_biofilm_absolute$Treatment <- factor(
  data.scores_biofilm_absolute$Treatment,
  levels = c("NC", "Pro", "Cip", "Mix"),
  labels  = c("Control", "Propyzamide", "Ciprofloxacin", "Mixture")
)

# Function to rescale coordinates
rescale_vectors <- function(df, scale_factor = 1) {
  df$length <- sqrt(df$NMDS1^2 + df$NMDS2^2)
  max_length <- max(df$length)
  df$NMDS1 <- df$NMDS1 / max_length * scale_factor
  df$NMDS2 <- df$NMDS2 / max_length * scale_factor
  df$length <- NULL
  return(df)
}

# Apply to each group
PUFAs_coord_cont_ba <- rescale_vectors(PUFAs_coord_cont_ba, 0.3)
MUFAs_coord_cont_ba <- rescale_vectors(MUFAs_coord_cont_ba, 0.3)
SAFAs_coord_cont_ba <- rescale_vectors(SAFAs_coord_cont_ba, 0.3)


# Compute convex hull for each Treatment
polygon_data_biofilm_treatment <- data.scores_biofilm_absolute %>%
  group_by(Treatment) %>%
  summarise(hull_points = list(data.frame(
    NMDS1 = NMDS1[chull(NMDS1, NMDS2)],
    NMDS2 = NMDS2[chull(NMDS1, NMDS2)]
  ))) %>%
  unnest(hull_points)

# plot
absol_biofilm_with_vectors = ggplot(data = data.scores_biofilm_absolute, aes(x = NMDS1, y = NMDS2)) +
  geom_polygon(
    data = polygon_data_biofilm_treatment,
    aes(
      x = NMDS1,
      y = NMDS2,
      group = Treatment,
      fill = Treatment
    ),
    alpha = 0.2,
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_point(aes(shape = Treatment, color = Treatment),
             size = 5,
             alpha = 1,
             show.legend = TRUE) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(
    "Control" = 16,
    "Propyzamide" = 17,
    "Ciprofloxacin" = 18,
    "Mixture" = 15
  )) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "Control" = "#009E73",
    "Propyzamide" = "#E69F00",
    "Ciprofloxacin" = "#56B4E9",
    "Mixture" = "#999999"
  )) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Control" = "#009E73",
    "Propyzamide" = "#E69F00",
    "Ciprofloxacin" = "#56B4E9",
    "Mixture" = "#999999"
  ))  +
  labs(color = "Treatment") +
  geom_segment(
    data = PUFAs_coord_cont_ba,
    aes(
      x = 0,
      y = 0,
      xend = NMDS1,
      yend = NMDS2
    ),
    linewidth = 0.5,
    alpha = 0.2,
    colour = "black"
  ) +
  geom_segment(
    data = MUFAs_coord_cont_ba,
    aes(
      x = 0,
      y = 0,
      xend = NMDS1,
      yend = NMDS2
    ),
    linewidth = 0.5,
    alpha = 0.2,
    colour = "black"
  ) +
  geom_segment(
    data = SAFAs_coord_cont_ba,
    aes(
      x = 0,
      y = 0,
      xend = NMDS1,
      yend = NMDS2
    ),
    linewidth = 0.5,
    alpha = 0.2,
    colour = "black"
  ) +
  geom_text_repel(
    data = PUFAs_coord_cont_ba,
    aes(
      x = NMDS1,
      y = NMDS2,
      label = row.names(PUFAs_coord_cont_ba)
    ),
    colour = "black",
    size = 2.5
  ) +
  geom_text_repel(
    data = MUFAs_coord_cont_ba,
    aes(
      x = NMDS1,
      y = NMDS2,
      label = row.names(MUFAs_coord_cont_ba)
    ),
    colour = "#996600",
    size = 2.5
  ) +
  geom_text_repel(
    data = SAFAs_coord_cont_ba,
    aes(
      x = NMDS1,
      y = NMDS2,
      label = row.names(SAFAs_coord_cont_ba)
    ),
    colour = "#0000FF",
    size = 2.5
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(
      size = 13,
      face = "bold",
      colour = "grey30"
    ),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA, colour = "grey30"),
    legend.key = element_blank(),
    legend.title = element_text(
      size = 11,
      face = "bold",
      colour = "grey30"
    ),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10, colour = "grey30"),
    plot.margin = margin(
      t = 0.2,
      b = 0.2,
      l = 0.2,
      r = 0.2,
      unit = "cm"
    ),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(colour = "grey95", size = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(colour = "grey95", size = 0.2)
  )

absol_biofilm_with_vectors

# position of FA names for publication were manually adjusted in vector graphic with the software Inkscape

Save the plot as .png:

ggsave(
  "output/NMDS_Biofilm_absolute.png",
  width = 16.5,
  height = 12,
  units = "cm",
  dpi = 600
)

Save the plot as vector graphic:

ggsave(
  "output/NMDS_Biofilm_absolute.svg",
  device = "svg",
  width = 16.5,
  height = 16,
  units = "cm",
  dpi = 600
)

4.4 PERMANOVA FA biofilm

Create distance matrix with fatty acids:

FA_biofilm_dist <- vegdist(FA_Biofilm_absolute_wide_sqrt, method = 'bray')

Separate explanatory variables from fatty acid information:

FA_biofilm_absolute_wide_permanova <-
  pivot_wider(FA_Biofilm_absolute,
              names_from = fa,
              values_from = fa_conc)

# square root transformation again, to reduce discriminatory power of dominant fa groups
FA_biofilm_absolute_wide_permanova[, 4:37] <- sqrt(FA_biofilm_absolute_wide_permanova[, 4:37])

# delete fa columns
FA_biofilm_absolute_wide_permanova <-
  FA_biofilm_absolute_wide_permanova[, c(1, 2, 3)]

FA_biofilm_absolute_wide_permanova$treatment <-
  as.factor(FA_biofilm_absolute_wide_permanova$treatment)

Perform PERMANOVA for treatments:

# perform PERMANOVA for treatments
perm_FA_biofilm_treatment <- adonis2(FA_biofilm_dist ~ treatment, data = FA_biofilm_absolute_wide_permanova, permutations = 999)
print(perm_FA_biofilm_treatment)
Permutation test for adonis under reduced model
Terms added sequentially (first to last)
Permutation: free
Number of permutations: 999

adonis2(formula = FA_biofilm_dist ~ treatment, data = FA_biofilm_absolute_wide_permanova, permutations = 999)
          Df SumOfSqs      R2      F Pr(>F)
treatment  3 0.031983 0.19625 0.6511  0.834
Residual   8 0.130991 0.80375              
Total     11 0.162975 1.00000

No significant differences between biofilm treatments. Anyway pairwise comparisons (by: Martinez Arbizu, P. (2020). pairwiseAdonis: Pairwise multilevel comparison using adonis. R package version 0.4):

pairwise_results_biofilm_treatment <- pairwise.adonis2(
  FA_biofilm_dist ~ treatment,
  data = FA_biofilm_absolute_wide_permanova,
  permutations = 99
)
Set of permutations < 'minperm'. Generating entire set.
Set of permutations < 'minperm'. Generating entire set.
Set of permutations < 'minperm'. Generating entire set.
Set of permutations < 'minperm'. Generating entire set.
Set of permutations < 'minperm'. Generating entire set.
Set of permutations < 'minperm'. Generating entire set.
# View the results
print(pairwise_results_biofilm_treatment)
$parent_call
[1] "FA_biofilm_dist ~ treatment , strata = Null , permutations 999"

$NC_vs_Cip
          Df SumOfSqs      R2      F Pr(>F)
treatment  1 0.005181 0.12336 0.5629   0.85
Residual   4 0.036820 0.87664              
Total      5 0.042002 1.00000              

$NC_vs_Pro
          Df SumOfSqs      R2      F Pr(>F)
treatment  1 0.017148 0.19118 0.9455   0.38
Residual   4 0.072544 0.80882              
Total      5 0.089692 1.00000              

$NC_vs_Mix
          Df SumOfSqs      R2      F Pr(>F)
treatment  1 0.015965 0.20739 1.0466   0.42
Residual   4 0.061013 0.79261              
Total      5 0.076978 1.00000              

$Cip_vs_Pro
          Df SumOfSqs      R2      F Pr(>F)
treatment  1 0.007215 0.09347 0.4124      1
Residual   4 0.069978 0.90653              
Total      5 0.077193 1.00000              

$Cip_vs_Mix
          Df SumOfSqs      R2      F Pr(>F)
treatment  1 0.006599 0.10145 0.4516   0.81
Residual   4 0.058447 0.89855              
Total      5 0.065046 1.00000              

$Pro_vs_Mix
          Df SumOfSqs      R2      F Pr(>F)
treatment  1 0.011859 0.11184 0.5037   0.88
Residual   4 0.094171 0.88816              
Total      5 0.106030 1.00000              

attr(,"class")
[1] "pwadstrata" "list"      
# Cip*Pro interaction?
# add presence / absence columns
FA_biofilm_absolute_wide_permanova$Cip <- ifelse(FA_biofilm_absolute_wide_permanova$treatment %in% c("Cip", "Mix"),
                                                 1,
                                                 0)

FA_biofilm_absolute_wide_permanova$Cip <- 
  factor(FA_biofilm_absolute_wide_permanova$Cip, levels = c(0, 1))

FA_biofilm_absolute_wide_permanova$Pro <- ifelse(FA_biofilm_absolute_wide_permanova$treatment %in% c("Pro", "Mix"),
                                                 1,
                                                 0)

FA_biofilm_absolute_wide_permanova$Pro <- 
  factor(FA_biofilm_absolute_wide_permanova$Pro, levels = c(0, 1))

# sanity check
table(FA_biofilm_absolute_wide_permanova$treatment,
      FA_biofilm_absolute_wide_permanova$Cip)
     
      0 1
  Cip 0 3
  Mix 0 3
  NC  3 0
  Pro 3 0
table(FA_biofilm_absolute_wide_permanova$treatment,
      FA_biofilm_absolute_wide_permanova$Pro)
     
      0 1
  Cip 3 0
  Mix 0 3
  NC  3 0
  Pro 0 3
# permanova
perm_FA_biofilm_Cip_Pro <- adonis2(FA_biofilm_dist ~ Cip * Pro,
        data = FA_biofilm_absolute_wide_permanova,
        permutations = 999)

print(perm_FA_biofilm_Cip_Pro)
Permutation test for adonis under reduced model
Terms added sequentially (first to last)
Permutation: free
Number of permutations: 999

adonis2(formula = FA_biofilm_dist ~ Cip * Pro, data = FA_biofilm_absolute_wide_permanova, permutations = 999)
         Df SumOfSqs      R2      F Pr(>F)
Cip       1 0.008237 0.05054 0.5030  0.843
Pro       1 0.014943 0.09169 0.9126  0.459
Cip:Pro   1 0.008803 0.05402 0.5376  0.834
Residual  8 0.130991 0.80375              
Total    11 0.162975 1.00000              
# no interaction

4.5 SIMPER FA biofilm

# create extra table with treatments
biofilm_treatments_table <- FA_biofilm_absolute_wide_permanova

View(biofilm_treatments_table)

# Perform SIMPER
simper_FA_biofilm_treatments <-
  with(
    biofilm_treatments_table,
    simper(FA_Biofilm_absolute_wide_sqrt, treatment,  permutations = 999)
  )

summary(simper_FA_biofilm_treatments)

---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
Permutation: free
Number of permutations: 999
simper_sum <- summary(simper_FA_biofilm_treatments)

# save results
out_dir <- "output"
dir.create(out_dir, showWarnings = FALSE)

safe_name <- function(x) gsub("[^A-Za-z0-9_]+", "_", x)

iwalk(simper_sum, function(x, nm) {
  if (is.null(x)) return(invisible(NULL))

  # SIMPER summary as a data.frame:
  # columns: average, sd, ratio, ava, avb, cumsum, p
  # rownames are the fatty acid IDs
  df <- as.data.frame(x)

  # keep rows up to the first that reaches/exceeds 70%
  idx <- which(df$cumsum >= 0.70)[1]
  if (is.na(idx)) idx <- nrow(df)
  out <- df[seq_len(idx), , drop = FALSE]

  # just keep 3 decimals
  out_write <- out
  num_cols <- vapply(out_write, is.numeric, logical(1))
  out_write[, num_cols] <- lapply(out_write[, num_cols, drop = FALSE],
                                  function(col) formatC(col, format = "f", digits = 3))

  file <- file.path(out_dir, paste0(safe_name(nm), "_top70.tsv"))
  # write row names so the TSV mirrors the SIMPER structure
  write.table(out_write, file, sep = "\t", quote = FALSE, col.names = NA)
})
# Combine results into a data frame
simper_results_biofilm_treatments <- do.call(rbind, lapply(simper_FA_biofilm_treatments, function(x) {
  # Check if the element contains valid data
  if (!is.null(x$p)) {
    data.frame(
      fatty_acid = names(x$p),  
      p = as.numeric(x$p),      
      average = as.numeric(x$average),  
      sd = as.numeric(x$sd),
      ratio = as.numeric(x$ratio),
      cusum = as.numeric(x$cusum),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  } else {
    NULL  # Skip if no p-values
  }
}))

# Show only significant results
simper_results_biofilm_treatments %>%
  dplyr::filter(p <= 0.05) %>%
  dplyr::select(fatty_acid, p, average, sd, ratio, cusum)  
           fatty_acid     p     average          sd    ratio     cusum
NC_Pro.5         16:0 0.007 0.024795062 0.013406967 1.849416 0.6313639
NC_Pro.6         16:1 0.033 0.014780551 0.011323999 1.305241 0.6796215
NC_Mix.17     20:4n-6 0.041 0.004599664 0.002487812 1.848879 0.9444498
Cip_Mix.11    18:3n-6 0.050 0.010905888 0.007363761 1.481021 0.7883126
Pro_Mix.11    18:3n-6 0.050 0.011401758 0.006013469 1.896037 0.8026106
# How often do they occur
simper_results_biofilm_treatments %>%
  dplyr::filter(p <= 0.05) %>%
  dplyr::count(fatty_acid) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(desc(n)) %>%
  print()
  fatty_acid n
1    18:3n-6 2
2       16:0 1
3       16:1 1
4    20:4n-6 1

5 Dryweight snails

Unit of soft body dry weight = mg

# insert data (Culture snails from in-house breeding)
dryweight_snails <-
  read.table(file.path("data\\dryweight_snails.txt"),
             header = TRUE, sep = "\t")

View(dryweight_snails)
# 3 snails from the 20 baseline (culture) snails were lost during the analyses.

5.1 Calculate mean dryweight, se and 95% CI of all treatment snails and the culture snails

mean_dryweight_snails <- dryweight_snails %>%
  group_by(treatment) %>%
  dplyr::summarize(
    mean_dryweight_snails = mean(dryweight_mg, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = sum(!is.na(dryweight_mg)),
    se = sd(dryweight_mg, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n),
    t_crit = qt(0.975, df = n - 1),
    ci_lower = mean_dryweight_snails - t_crit * se,
    ci_upper = mean_dryweight_snails + t_crit * se,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(treatment, mean_dryweight_snails, ci_lower, ci_upper, se)
  
print(mean_dryweight_snails)
# A tibble: 5 × 5
  treatment mean_dryweight_snails ci_lower ci_upper     se
  <chr>                     <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>  <dbl>
1 Cip                       0.534    0.464    0.604 0.0309
2 Culture                   0.439    0.378    0.499 0.0286
3 Mix                       0.507    0.443    0.572 0.0286
4 NC                        0.679    0.582    0.775 0.0426
5 Pro                       0.553    0.483    0.623 0.0309

Snails from feeding assay (fed with biofilm) on average heavier than snails from culture (fed with Tetraphyll flakes).

#culture/baseline snails
culture_snails <- dryweight_snails %>%
  dplyr::filter(c(treatment == "Culture"))

hist(culture_snails $ dryweight_mg) 

skewness(culture_snails $ dryweight_mg, na.rm = TRUE) 
[1] 0.2135628
shapiro.test(culture_snails $ dryweight_mg)

    Shapiro-Wilk normality test

data:  culture_snails$dryweight_mg
W = 0.93454, p-value = 0.259
# culture / baseline snails normal distributed

# calculate differences in final dry weight in comparison to culture snails
percentage_differences <- dryweight_snails %>%
  group_by(treatment) %>%
  dplyr::summarize(mean_dryweight = mean(dryweight_mg)) %>%
  mutate(percentage_differences = (mean_dryweight / mean_dryweight[treatment == "Culture"]) * 100 - 100)

percentage_differences
# A tibble: 5 × 3
  treatment mean_dryweight percentage_differences
  <chr>              <dbl>                  <dbl>
1 Cip                0.534                   21.8
2 Culture            0.439                    0  
3 Mix                0.507                   15.7
4 NC                 0.679                   54.8
5 Pro                0.553                   26.1

5.2 Histogram

# filter culture snails
filtered_dry_weight <- dryweight_snails %>%
  filter(!treatment %in% c("Culture"))

hist(filtered_dry_weight $ dryweight_mg) 

skewness(filtered_dry_weight $ dryweight_mg, na.rm = TRUE) 
[1] 0.5211954
shapiro.test(filtered_dry_weight $ dryweight_mg)

    Shapiro-Wilk normality test

data:  filtered_dry_weight$dryweight_mg
W = 0.9753, p-value = 0.52

Soft tissue dry weights normal distributed.

5.3 Model dry weight snails

# explanatory variable treatment as factor
filtered_dry_weight $ treatment <- factor(filtered_dry_weight $ treatment)
  
model_snails <- lm(dryweight_mg ~ treatment, data = filtered_dry_weight)
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
plot(model_snails) # normal distribution assumed

qreference(residuals(model_snails)) 

drop1(model_snails, test = 'F')
Single term deletions

Model:
dryweight_mg ~ treatment
          Df Sum of Sq     RSS     AIC F value   Pr(>F)   
<none>                 0.40937 -175.28                    
treatment  3   0.17317 0.58255 -167.17  5.0763 0.004928 **
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

Explanatory variable “treatment” highly significant.

dw_snails_inter <- lsmeans(model_snails, ~ treatment)

dw_snails_inter_dunn1 <- contrast(dw_snails_inter, 'pairwise')

dw_snails_inter_dunn_sum1 <- summary(dw_snails_inter_dunn1, infer = TRUE, adjust = 'mvt')

dw_snails_inter_dunn1
 contrast  estimate     SE df t.ratio p.value
 Cip - Mix   0.0269 0.0477 36   0.564  0.9422
 Cip - NC   -0.1445 0.0477 36  -3.031  0.0223
 Cip - Pro  -0.0186 0.0477 36  -0.391  0.9794
 Mix - NC   -0.1714 0.0477 36  -3.595  0.0051
 Mix - Pro  -0.0455 0.0477 36  -0.955  0.7757
 NC - Pro    0.1259 0.0477 36   2.640  0.0564

P value adjustment: tukey method for comparing a family of 4 estimates

Significant difference between dry weight of Cip and Mix snails compared to NC; almost significant difference between Pro treatment and NC.

Test for interaction effects of Cip and Pro:

# interaction effect model with presence / absence of single xenobiotics
filtered_dry_weight$Cip <- ifelse(filtered_dry_weight$treatment %in% c("Cip", "Mix"),
                                1, 0)

filtered_dry_weight$Cip <- factor(filtered_dry_weight$Cip, levels = c(0, 1))

filtered_dry_weight$Pro <- ifelse(filtered_dry_weight$treatment %in% c("Pro", "Mix"),
                                1, 0)

filtered_dry_weight$Pro <- factor(filtered_dry_weight$Pro, levels = c(0, 1))

# sanity check
table(filtered_dry_weight$treatment,
      filtered_dry_weight$Cip,
      filtered_dry_weight$Pro)
, ,  = 0

     
       0  1
  Cip  0 10
  Mix  0  0
  NC  10  0
  Pro  0  0

, ,  = 1

     
       0  1
  Cip  0  0
  Mix  0 10
  NC   0  0
  Pro 10  0
# interaction model
model_dryweight_interact <- lm(dryweight_mg ~ Cip * Pro, data = filtered_dry_weight)
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
plot(model_dryweight_interact) # point cloud looks evenly distributed --> normal distribution

qreference(residuals(model_dryweight_interact)) 

drop1(model_dryweight_interact, test = 'F')
Single term deletions

Model:
dryweight_mg ~ Cip * Pro
        Df Sum of Sq     RSS     AIC F value Pr(>F)
<none>               0.40937 -175.28               
Cip:Pro  1  0.024512 0.43388 -174.95  2.1556 0.1507
# Cip:Pro interaction not significant
# df.ref only control values
df.ref.dryweight<- filtered_dry_weight |>
  dplyr::filter(Cip == 0, Pro == 0) 

nd_dryweight_snails <- nd.generator(df.ref.dryweight, stressor1 = "Cip", stressor2 = "Pro", diff = NULL)

#sanity check  
table(nd_dryweight_snails$dataset, nd_dryweight_snails$Cip)
     
       0  1
  C   10  0
  S1   0 10
  S12  0 10
  S2  10  0
table(nd_dryweight_snails$dataset, nd_dryweight_snails$Pro)
     
       0  1
  C   10  0
  S1  10  0
  S12  0 10
  S2   0 10
#check for AD, MU (all values >0) and OD
hyp_all_dryweight_snails <- predictions(
  model_dryweight_interact,
  newdata = nd_dryweight_snails,
  hypothesis = function(x) {
    hypothesis.function(
      x,
      stressor1 = "Cip",
      stressor2 = "Pro",
      null = "all",        # <- AD, MU, DO together
      strategy = "average"
    )
  }
) |>
  inferences(method = "simulation")

hyp_all_dryweight_snails

 Term Estimate   2.5 % 97.5 %
   ad   0.0990 -0.0309 0.2324
   do  -0.0269 -0.0964 0.0753
   mu   0.1535 -0.0869 0.3988

Type: response
hyp_all_dryweight_snails$term <- dplyr::recode(hyp_all_dryweight_snails$term,
                                    "ad" = "AD",
                                    "do" = "DO",
                                    "mu" = "MU")

ggplot(hyp_all_dryweight_snails, aes(x = term, y = estimate)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high), width = 0.2) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(x = "Null model", y = "Interaction estimate")

Both ciprofloxacin and propyzamide significantly affected snail dry weight in the additive model, whereas the interaction term was not significant. Post-estimation inference following Pimentel et al. (2025) showed that the additive, multiplicative, and dominance null models could not be rejected, indicating that the observed mixture response did not deviate significantly from any of these expectations. Together, these results suggest that the effects of both stressors on snail dry weight were largely independent and did not exhibit a detectable interaction.

5.4 Boxplot dry weight snails

# Plot
snails_boxplot <-
  ggplot(data = filtered_dry_weight,
         mapping = aes(x = factor(
           treatment, levels = c("NC", "Pro", "Cip", "Mix")
         ), y = dryweight_mg)) +
  # Mean
  stat_summary(
    fun = mean,
    geom = "point",
    aes(fill = treatment),
    shape = 21,
    size = 5,
    color = "grey20",
    stroke = 0.5
  ) +
  
  # 95% CI
  # Black outline (thicker, underneath)
  stat_summary(
    fun.data = mean_cl_normal,
    geom = "errorbar",
    aes(group = treatment),
    width = 0.2,
    linewidth = 1,
    color = "grey30"
  ) +
  
  # Colored CI on top
  stat_summary(
    fun.data = mean_cl_normal,
    geom = "errorbar",
    aes(color = treatment),
    width = 0.2,
    linewidth = 0.8
  ) +
  
  # Jittered
geom_point(
  aes(color = treatment),
  position = position_jitter(width = 0.25, height = 0),
  alpha = 0.8,
  size = 2
) +
  
  # Color scales
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "NC" = "#009E73",
    "Pro" = "#E69F00",
    "Cip" = "#56B4E9",
    "Mix" = "#999999"
  )) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "NC" = "#009E73",
    "Pro" = "#E69F00",
    "Cip" = "#56B4E9",
    "Mix" = "#999999"
  )) +
  guides(fill = "none") +
  
  # Theme and labels
  labs(x = "Treatment", y = "Grazer Dry Weight (mg)", color = "") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Control", "Propyzamide", "Ciprofloxacin", "Mixture")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "grey100"),
    axis.title = element_text(
      size = 13,
      face = "bold",
      colour = "grey30"
    ),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12),  
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
    panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA, colour = "grey30"),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(colour = "grey85", size = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(colour = "grey95", size = 0.2)
  ) +
  # Significance annotations
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 1.5,
    y = max(filtered_dry_weight$dryweight_mg) + 0.07,
    label = "p=0.056",
    size = 4,
    color = "grey30"
  ) +
  geom_segment(
    aes(
      x = 1,
      xend = 2,
      y = max(dryweight_mg) + 0.05,
      yend = max(dryweight_mg) + 0.05
    ),
    lineend = "round",
    linewidth = 0.5,
    color = "grey30"
  ) +
  # Vertical tick on the left 
  geom_segment(
    aes(
      x = 1,
      xend = 1,
      y = max(dryweight_mg) + 0.04,
      yend = max(dryweight_mg) + 0.05
    ),
    color = "grey30",
    linewidth = 0.5
  ) +
  # # Vertical tick on the right 
  geom_segment(
    aes(
      x = 2,
      xend = 2,
      y = max(dryweight_mg) + 0.04,
      yend = max(dryweight_mg) + 0.05
    ),
    color = "grey30",
    linewidth = 0.5
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 2.5,
    y = max(filtered_dry_weight$dryweight_mg) + 0.17,
    label = "p=0.005**",
    size = 4,
    color = "grey30"
  ) +
  geom_segment(
    aes(
      x = 1,
      xend = 4,
      y = max(dryweight_mg) + 0.15,
      yend = max(dryweight_mg) + 0.15
    ),
    lineend = "round",
    linewidth = 0.5,
    color = "grey30"
  ) +
  geom_segment(
    aes(
      x = 1,
      xend = 1,
      y = max(dryweight_mg) + 0.14,
      yend = max(dryweight_mg) + 0.15
    ),
    color = "grey30",
    linewidth = 0.5
  ) +
  geom_segment(
    aes(
      x = 4,
      xend = 4,
      y = max(dryweight_mg) + 0.14,
      yend = max(dryweight_mg) + 0.15
    ),
    color = "grey30",
    linewidth = 0.5
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 2,
    y = max(filtered_dry_weight$dryweight_mg) + 0.12,
    label = "p=0.022*",
    size = 4,
    color = "grey30"
  ) +
  geom_segment(
    aes(
      x = 1,
      xend = 3,
      y = max(dryweight_mg) + 0.1,
      yend = max(dryweight_mg) + 0.1
    ),
    lineend = "round",
    linewidth = 0.5,
    color = "grey30"
  ) +
  geom_segment(
    aes(
      x = 1,
      xend = 1,
      y = max(dryweight_mg) + 0.09,
      yend = max(dryweight_mg) + 0.1
    ),
    color = "grey30",
    linewidth = 0.5
  ) +
  geom_segment(
    aes(
      x = 3,
      xend = 3,
      y = max(dryweight_mg) + 0.09,
      yend = max(dryweight_mg) + 0.1
    ),
    color = "grey30",
    linewidth = 0.5
  )


snails_boxplot

# save the plot
ggsave(
  "output/snails_boxplot.png",
  width = 16.5,
  height = 12,
  units = "cm",
  dpi = 600
)

6 Feeding rate snails

  • NA’s due to missing snail and zero values of feeding rate due to no measurable feeding in respective week

  • Unit feeding rate: µg / day

  • Column “used_for_FA_analysis” highlights the ten snails used for FA analyses for each treatment

# insert data
afdw_feeding_rate <-
  read.table(file.path("data\\feeding_assay_afdw_feeding_rate.txt"),
             header = TRUE, sep = "\t")

View(afdw_feeding_rate)

6.1 Feeding rate snails

6.1.0.1 Histogram feeding rate

hist(afdw_feeding_rate $ feeding_rate) 

skewness(afdw_feeding_rate $ feeding_rate, na.rm = TRUE) # skewness > 3
[1] 3.865973
shapiro.test(afdw_feeding_rate $ feeding_rate) # not normal distributed

    Shapiro-Wilk normality test

data:  afdw_feeding_rate$feeding_rate
W = 0.54728, p-value < 2.2e-16
# following visual observation of absolute data & histogram, check for outliers

Outlier check:

#Run function one time, after that you can use the shortcut outlierKD (your subset, variable)
outlierKD_feeding_rate <- function(dt, id_col = "treatment", start_col = 6, end_col = 6) {
  # Initialize an empty list to store results for each column
  outlier_results <- list()
  
  
  for (i in start_col:end_col) {
    col_name <- colnames(dt)[i]  # Get the column name
    
    # Extract the column values
    var_name <- dt[[col_name]]
    
    # Check if the column is numeric
    if (is.numeric(var_name)) {
      na1 <- sum(is.na(var_name))
      m1 <- mean(var_name, na.rm = TRUE)
      
      # Identify outliers using boxplot stats
      outlier <- boxplot.stats(var_name)$out  
      
      # Find rows with outliers
      outlier_rows <- which(var_name %in% outlier)
      
      # If no outliers, skip the rest of the loop
      if (length(outlier_rows) == 0) next
      
      # Get replicate values for the outliers
      id_values <- dt[[id_col]][outlier_rows]
      
      # Mean of outliers
      mo <- mean(outlier)
      
      # Collect the outlier information for this column
      outlier_results[[col_name]] <- data.frame(
        Column = col_name,
        Row = outlier_rows,
        Replicate = id_values,
        Outlier = outlier
      )
      
      cat("\nOutliers for column:", col_name, "\n")
      cat("Outliers identified:", length(outlier), "\n")
      cat("Proportion (%) of outliers:", 
          round(length(outlier) / sum(!is.na(var_name)) * 100, 1), "\n")
      cat("Mean of the outliers:", round(mo, 2), "\n")
      cat("Mean with outliers:", round(m1, 2), "\n")
    }
  }
  
  # Combine all outlier information into one data frame
  if (length(outlier_results) > 0) {
    combined_outliers <- do.call(rbind, outlier_results)
  } else {
    combined_outliers <- data.frame()  # If no outliers were found, return an empty data frame
  }
  
  # Return combined data frame with outliers information
  return(combined_outliers)
}

# Call the function and store the results
outlier_info_combined_feeding_rate <- outlierKD_feeding_rate(afdw_feeding_rate, id_col = "treatment", start_col = 5, end_col = 5)

Outliers for column: feeding_rate 
Outliers identified: 38 
Proportion (%) of outliers: 11.5 
Mean of the outliers: 14.87 
Mean with outliers: 3.05 
# Print the combined outlier information
print(outlier_info_combined_feeding_rate)
                      Column Row Replicate   Outlier
feeding_rate.1  feeding_rate  23        NC 45.410863
feeding_rate.2  feeding_rate  26        NC 31.850622
feeding_rate.3  feeding_rate  28        NC  9.967544
feeding_rate.4  feeding_rate  33       Pro 12.372582

feeding_rate.14 feeding_rate  44       Pro 15.941264
feeding_rate.15 feeding_rate  45       Pro 13.484484

feeding_rate.17 feeding_rate  48       Pro  9.594064
feeding_rate.18 feeding_rate  60       Pro  9.878984

feeding_rate.22 feeding_rate 118       Mix  7.739009

feeding_rate.24 feeding_rate 131        NC  7.543135
feeding_rate.25 feeding_rate 132        NC  8.173696
feeding_rate.26 feeding_rate 135        NC  6.728001
feeding_rate.27 feeding_rate 142        NC 11.356729
feeding_rate.28 feeding_rate 144        NC 13.312560
feeding_rate.29 feeding_rate 182       Cip  8.006604
feeding_rate.30 feeding_rate 190       Cip 10.259977
feeding_rate.31 feeding_rate 247        NC  7.966650
feeding_rate.32 feeding_rate 271       Pro 23.456943
feeding_rate.33 feeding_rate 277       Pro  6.978045
