# Assuming outlier_info_combined is the combined outlier data frame
outlier_info_combined_feeding_rate <- outlier_info_combined_feeding_rate  # If not already assigned

# Count the number of outliers for each replicate
outlier_counts_feeding_rate <- table(outlier_info_combined_feeding_rate$Replicate)

# Convert to a data frame for better readability
outlier_counts_feeding_rate_df <- as.data.frame(outlier_counts_feeding_rate)
colnames(outlier_counts_feeding_rate_df) <- c("Replicate", "Outlier_Count")

View(outlier_counts_feeding_rate_df)

Remove outliers:

# Extract the row numbers of the outliers
outlier_rows_feeding_rate <- outlier_info_combined_feeding_rate$Row

# Remove the outlier rows from the dataset
afdw_feeding_rate_cleaned_1 <- afdw_feeding_rate[-outlier_rows_feeding_rate, ]
# 38 outliers identified and removed

# remove NAs
afdw_feeding_rate_cleaned_1 <- na.omit(afdw_feeding_rate_cleaned_1)
# 30 NAs removed

Histogram feeding rate again

hist(afdw_feeding_rate_cleaned_1 $ feeding_rate) 

skewness(afdw_feeding_rate_cleaned_1 $ feeding_rate, na.rm = TRUE) 
[1] 1.352644
shapiro.test(afdw_feeding_rate_cleaned_1 $ feeding_rate)

    Shapiro-Wilk normality test

data:  afdw_feeding_rate_cleaned_1$feeding_rate
W = 0.87441, p-value = 1.013e-14

Feeding rate still not normal distributed, right skewed but skewness < 1.5, so no data transformation for model.

Calculate mean feeding rate + se + 95 CI for each treatment

mean_feeding_rate <- afdw_feeding_rate_cleaned_1 %>%
  group_by(treatment) %>%
  dplyr::summarize(
    mean_feeding = mean(feeding_rate, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = sum(!is.na(feeding_rate)),
    se = sd(feeding_rate, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n),
    t_crit = qt(0.975, df = n - 1),
    ci_lower = mean_feeding - t_crit * se,
    ci_upper = mean_feeding + t_crit * se,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(treatment, mean_feeding, ci_lower, ci_upper, se)

print(mean_feeding_rate)
# A tibble: 4 × 5
  treatment mean_feeding ci_lower ci_upper    se
  <chr>            <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> <dbl>
1 Cip               1.62    1.26      1.98 0.179
2 Mix               1.20    0.989     1.41 0.106
3 NC                1.76    1.44      2.08 0.161
4 Pro               1.54    1.14      1.93 0.198

6.1.0.2 Model feeding rate

# treatment and beaker as factor
afdw_feeding_rate_cleaned_1 $ treatment <- factor(afdw_feeding_rate_cleaned_1 $ treatment)
afdw_feeding_rate_cleaned_1 $ beaker <- factor(afdw_feeding_rate_cleaned_1 $ beaker)

# response variable: feeding_rate
# fixed effects: treatment
# random effect with constant intercept: (1|beaker)
feeding_rate_lmer <- lmer(feeding_rate ~ treatment + (1|beaker), data = afdw_feeding_rate_cleaned_1)
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
plot(feeding_rate_lmer) 

qreference(residuals(feeding_rate_lmer)) # rather S shape

pairwise.wilcox.test(afdw_feeding_rate_cleaned_1$feeding_rate,
                     afdw_feeding_rate_cleaned_1$treatment,
                     p.adjust.method = "holm")

    Pairwise comparisons using Wilcoxon rank sum test with continuity correction 

data:  afdw_feeding_rate_cleaned_1$feeding_rate and afdw_feeding_rate_cleaned_1$treatment 

    Cip   Mix   NC   
Mix 1.000 -     -    
NC  1.000 0.059 -    
Pro 1.000 1.000 0.738

P value adjustment method: holm 
# trend NC:Mix p = 0.059

Mix vs. Control trend p = 0.059

Ciprofloxacin x propyzamide interaction

afdw_feeding_rate_cleaned_1$Cip <- ifelse(afdw_feeding_rate_cleaned_1$treatment %in% c("Cip", "Mix"),
                                1, 0)

afdw_feeding_rate_cleaned_1$Cip <- factor(afdw_feeding_rate_cleaned_1$Cip, levels = c(0, 1))

afdw_feeding_rate_cleaned_1$Pro <- ifelse(afdw_feeding_rate_cleaned_1$treatment %in% c("Pro", "Mix"),
                                1, 0)

afdw_feeding_rate_cleaned_1$Pro <- factor(afdw_feeding_rate_cleaned_1$Pro, levels = c(0, 1))

# sanity check
table(afdw_feeding_rate_cleaned_1$treatment,
      afdw_feeding_rate_cleaned_1$Cip,
      afdw_feeding_rate_cleaned_1$Pro)
, ,  = 0

     
       0  1
  Cip  0 72
  Mix  0  0
  NC  74  0
  Pro  0  0

, ,  = 1

     
       0  1
  Cip  0  0
  Mix  0 86
  NC   0  0
  Pro 60  0
# interaction model
model_feeding_rate_interact <- lm(feeding_rate ~ Cip * Pro, data = afdw_feeding_rate_cleaned_1)
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
plot(model_feeding_rate_interact) # not normal distributed, S shape

qreference(residuals(model_feeding_rate_interact))

kruskal_feeding_rate_interaction <- afdw_feeding_rate_cleaned_1 %>%
  dplyr::mutate(treatment = interaction(Cip, Pro, sep = "_")) %>%
  kruskal.test(feeding_rate ~ treatment, data = .)

kruskal_feeding_rate_interaction

    Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test

data:  feeding_rate by treatment
Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 5.8446, df = 3, p-value = 0.1194

6.1.0.3 Boxplot feeding rate

# Plot
feeding_rate_boxplot <-
  ggplot(data = afdw_feeding_rate_cleaned_1,
         mapping = aes(x = factor(
           treatment, levels = c("NC", "Pro", "Cip", "Mix")
         ), y = feeding_rate)) +
  # Mean
  stat_summary(
    fun = mean,
    geom = "point",
    aes(fill = treatment),
    shape = 21,
    size = 5,
    color = "grey20",
    stroke = 0.5
  ) +
  
  # 95% CI
  # Black outline (thicker, underneath)
  stat_summary(
    fun.data = mean_cl_normal,
    geom = "errorbar",
    aes(group = treatment),
    width = 0.2,
    linewidth = 1,
    color = "grey30"
  ) +
  
  # Colored CI on top
  stat_summary(
    fun.data = mean_cl_normal,
    geom = "errorbar",
    aes(color = treatment),
    width = 0.2,
    linewidth = 0.8
  ) +
  
  # Jittered
geom_point(
  aes(color = treatment),
  position = position_jitter(width = 0.25, height = 0),
  alpha = 0.8,
  size = 2
) +
  
  # Color scales
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "NC" = "#009E73",
    "Pro" = "#E69F00",
    "Cip" = "#56B4E9",
    "Mix" = "#999999"
  )) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "NC" = "#009E73",
    "Pro" = "#E69F00",
    "Cip" = "#56B4E9",
    "Mix" = "#999999"
  )) +
  guides(fill = "none") +
  
  # Theme and labels
  labs(x = "Treatment", y = "Grazer Feeding Rate (µg / day)", color = "") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Control", "Propyzamide", "Ciprofloxacin", "Mixture")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "grey100"),
    axis.title = element_text(
      size = 13,
      face = "bold",
      colour = "grey30"
    ),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12),  
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
    panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA, colour = "grey30"),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(colour = "grey85", size = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(colour = "grey95", size = 0.2)
  )

  "output/feeding_rate.png",
  width = 16.5,
  height = 12,

7 Fatty acid composition snails

Statistics for fatty acid composition of snails in absolute values (µg fatty acid / mg dry weight snail (soft body)).

# insert data
FA_Snails_absolute <-
  read.table(file.path("data\\FA_Snails_absolute.txt"),
             header = TRUE, sep = "\t")

View(FA_Snails_absolute)
# Mix S_29 no data, fatty acid extraction did not work

7.1 Absolute SAFA, MUFA & PUFA concentrations

1) Total SAFA content

# total SAFA, MUFA, PUFA content; sa = "snail absolute" 
# total SAFA content 
# filter saturated fatty acids in new table 
SAFA_sa <- FA_Snails_absolute %>%
filter(fa %in% c("C08:0", "C10:0", "C11:0", "C12:0", "C13:0", "C14:0", "C15:0", "C16:0", "C18:0", "C20:0", "C21:0", "C22:0", "C24:0"))

# Sum SAFA per replicate and treatment
total_SAFA_per_snail <- SAFA_sa %>% 
  dplyr::group_by(replicate, treatment) %>% 
  dplyr::summarize(sum_SAFA = sum(fa_conc), .groups = "drop") 
print(total_SAFA_per_snail) 
# A tibble: 39 × 3
   replicate treatment sum_SAFA
   <chr>     <chr>        <dbl>
 1 S_1       NC           287. 
 2 S_10      NC           102. 
 3 S_11      Pro          177. 
 4 S_12      Pro          171. 
 5 S_13      Pro          131. 
 6 S_14      Pro          128. 
 7 S_15      Pro           92.6
 8 S_16      Pro          102. 
 9 S_17      Pro          128. 
10 S_18      Pro          122. 
# ℹ 29 more rows
# Statistical differences
hist(total_SAFA_per_snail $ sum_SAFA) # normal distributed

shapiro.test(total_SAFA_per_snail $ sum_SAFA) # normal distributed

    Shapiro-Wilk normality test

data:  total_SAFA_per_snail$sum_SAFA
W = 0.96987, p-value = 0.372
leveneTest(sum_SAFA ~ treatment, data = total_SAFA_per_snail) # homogenous
Levene's Test for Homogeneity of Variance (center = median)
      Df F value Pr(>F)
group  3  0.5795 0.6324
      35               
anova_SAFA <- aov(sum_SAFA ~ treatment, data = total_SAFA_per_snail)
summary(anova_SAFA)
            Df Sum Sq Mean Sq F value  Pr(>F)    
treatment    3  38898   12966    9.26 0.00012 ***
Residuals   35  49007    1400                    
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
TukeyHSD(anova_SAFA)
  Tukey multiple comparisons of means
    95% family-wise confidence level

Fit: aov(formula = sum_SAFA ~ treatment, data = total_SAFA_per_snail)

$treatment
              diff        lwr        upr     p adj
Mix-Cip -74.922274 -121.28987 -28.554679 0.0006092
NC-Cip  -42.670799  -87.80170   2.460105 0.0694651
Pro-Cip -78.088463 -123.21937 -32.957559 0.0002465
NC-Mix   32.251476  -14.11612  78.619072 0.2567096
Pro-Mix  -3.166189  -49.53378  43.201407 0.9977405
Pro-NC  -35.417664  -80.54857   9.713240 0.1677967
# Calculate mean, se and 95% CI per treatment
mean_SAFA_sa <- total_SAFA_per_snail %>%
  dplyr::group_by(treatment) %>%
  dplyr::summarize(
    mean_SAFA = mean(sum_SAFA, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = sum(!is.na(sum_SAFA)),
    se = sd(sum_SAFA, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n),
    t_crit = qt(0.975, df = n - 1),
    ci_lower = mean_SAFA - t_crit * se,
    ci_upper = mean_SAFA + t_crit * se,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(treatment, mean_SAFA, ci_lower, ci_upper, se)

# Extract mean of control group for percental difference
control_mean_SAFA_sa <- mean_SAFA_sa %>%
  filter(treatment == "NC") %>%
  pull(mean_SAFA)

# Calculate percental difference from control
mean_SAFA_sa <- mean_SAFA_sa %>%
  mutate(percent_diff_from_NC = 100 * (mean_SAFA - control_mean_SAFA_sa) / control_mean_SAFA_sa)

print(mean_SAFA_sa)
# A tibble: 4 × 6
  treatment mean_SAFA ci_lower ci_upper    se percent_diff_from_NC
  <chr>         <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> <dbl>                <dbl>
1 Cip            215.     197.     233.  7.99                 24.8
2 Mix            140.     110.     170. 13.0                 -18.7
3 NC             172.     136.     208. 16.1                   0  
4 Pro            137.     116.     158.  9.34                -20.6

2) Total MUFA content

# filter mono-unsaturated fatty acids in new table
MUFA_sa <- FA_Snails_absolute %>%
  filter(fa %in% c("C14:1", "C15:1", "C16:1", "C17:1", "C18:1", "C20:1", "C22:1", "C24:1"))

# Sum MUFA per replicate and treatment
total_MUFA_per_snail <- MUFA_sa %>% 
  dplyr::group_by(replicate, treatment) %>% 
  dplyr::summarize(sum_MUFA = sum(fa_conc), .groups = "drop") 
print(total_MUFA_per_snail)

# Calculate mean, se and 95% CI per treatment
mean_MUFA_sa <- total_MUFA_per_snail %>%
  dplyr::group_by(treatment) %>%
  dplyr::summarize(
    mean_MUFA = mean(sum_MUFA, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = sum(!is.na(sum_MUFA)),
    se = sd(sum_MUFA, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n),
    t_crit = qt(0.975, df = n - 1),
    ci_lower = mean_MUFA - t_crit * se,
    ci_upper = mean_MUFA + t_crit * se,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(treatment, mean_MUFA, ci_lower, ci_upper, se)

# Extract mean of control group for percental difference
control_mean_MUFA_sa <- mean_MUFA_sa %>%
  filter(treatment == "NC") %>%
  pull(mean_MUFA)

# Calculate percental difference from control
mean_MUFA_sa <- mean_MUFA_sa %>%
  mutate(percent_diff_from_NC = 100 * (mean_MUFA - control_mean_MUFA_sa) / control_mean_MUFA_sa)

print(mean_MUFA_sa)

3) Total PUFA content

# filter poly-unsaturated fatty acids in new table
PUFA_sa <- FA_Snails_absolute %>%
  filter(fa %in% c("C18:2(n-6c)", "C18:2(n-6t)", "C18:3(n-3)", "C18:3(n-6)", "C18:4(n-3)", "C20:2(n-6)", "C20:3(n-6)", "C20:3(n-3)", "C20:4(n-6)", "C20:5(n-3)", "C22:2", "C22:5(n-3)", "C22:6(n-3)"))
  
# Sum PUFA per replicate and treatment
total_PUFA_per_snail <- PUFA_sa %>% 
  dplyr::group_by(replicate, treatment) %>% 
  dplyr::summarize(sum_PUFA = sum(fa_conc), .groups = "drop") 
print(total_PUFA_per_snail)

# Calculate mean, se and 95% CI per treatment
mean_PUFA_sa <- total_PUFA_per_snail %>%
  dplyr::group_by(treatment) %>%
  dplyr::summarize(
    mean_PUFA = mean(sum_PUFA, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = sum(!is.na(sum_PUFA)),
    se = sd(sum_PUFA, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n),
    t_crit = qt(0.975, df = n - 1),
    ci_lower = mean_PUFA - t_crit * se,
    ci_upper = mean_PUFA + t_crit * se,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(treatment, mean_PUFA, ci_lower, ci_upper, se)

# Extract mean of control group for percental difference
control_mean_PUFA_sa <- mean_PUFA_sa %>%
  filter(treatment == "NC") %>%
  pull(mean_PUFA)

# Calculate percental difference from control

  mutate(percent_diff_from_NC = 100 * (mean_PUFA - control_mean_PUFA_sa) / control_mean_PUFA_sa)

print(mean_PUFA_sa)

  treatment mean_PUFA ci_lower ci_upper    se percent_diff_from_NC

1 Cip            72.0     57.3     86.6  6.47                -24.2
2 Mix            51.7     39.7     63.6  5.18                -45.6
3 NC             95.0     78.5    111.   7.27                  0  
4 Pro            65.7     50.1     81.3  6.90                -30.8

SAFA, MUFA & PUFA relative differences to control plot:

snail_class_abs <- bind_rows(
  prep_class_from_replicates(total_SAFA_per_snail, "sum_SAFA", "SAFA"),
  prep_class_from_replicates(total_MUFA_per_snail, "sum_MUFA", "MUFA"),
  prep_class_from_replicates(total_PUFA_per_snail, "sum_PUFA", "PUFA")
) %>%
  mutate(
    Class = factor(Class, levels = c("SAFA","PUFA","MUFA")),
    treatment = factor(treatment, levels = c("NC","Pro","Cip","Mix"))
  )

snail_class_abs
# A tibble: 12 × 5
   treatment Class  mean   lwr   upr
   <fct>     <fct> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>
 1 Cip       SAFA  215.  197.  233. 
 2 Mix       SAFA  140.  110.  170. 
 3 NC        SAFA  172.  136.  208. 
 4 Pro       SAFA  137.  116.  158. 
 5 Cip       MUFA  143.  127.  158. 
 6 Mix       MUFA   77.5  47.6 107. 
 7 NC        MUFA  146.  130.  162. 
 8 Pro       MUFA  108.   65.8 150. 
 9 Cip       PUFA   72.0  57.3  86.6
10 Mix       PUFA   51.7  39.7  63.6
11 NC        PUFA   95.0  78.5 111. 
12 Pro       PUFA   65.7  50.1  81.3
# relative values
snail_class_rel <- snail_class_abs %>%
  group_by(Class) %>%
  mutate(
    ctrl_mean = mean[treatment == "NC"][1],
    mean = mean / ctrl_mean,
    lwr  = lwr  / ctrl_mean,
    upr  = upr  / ctrl_mean
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  dplyr::select(-ctrl_mean)


plot_df_snails <- snail_class_rel  

plot_df_snails <- plot_df_snails %>%
  mutate(
    show = ifelse(treatment == "NC", 0, 1),  
    treatment = factor(treatment, levels = c("NC","Pro","Cip","Mix")),
    Class = factor(Class, levels = c("PUFA", "MUFA", "SAFA")),
    lwr = ifelse(lwr < 0, 0, lwr)
  )

# plot
fa_snails_relative_changes <- ggplot(plot_df_snails, aes(x = mean, y = Class)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1,
             linewidth = 1,
             colour = "grey30") +
  
  # Black CI outline
  geom_errorbarh(
    aes(xmin = lwr, xmax = upr, group = treatment, alpha = show),
    height = 0,
    linewidth = 1,
    colour = "grey30",
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.45)
  ) +
  
  # Colored CI overlay
  geom_errorbarh(
    aes(xmin = lwr, xmax = upr, colour = treatment, alpha = show),
    height = 0,
    linewidth = 0.8,
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.45)
  ) +
  
  geom_point(
    aes(colour = treatment, alpha = show),
    size = 4,
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.45)
  ) +
  
  scale_colour_manual(
    values = c(
    "NC" = "#009E73",
    "Pro" = "#E69F00",
    "Cip" = "#56B4E9",
    "Mix" = "#999999"
    ),
    labels = c(
      "NC"  = "Control",
      "Pro" = "Propyzamide",
      "Cip" = "Ciprofloxacin",
      "Mix" = "Mixture"
    ),
    name = "Treatment"
  ) +
  scale_alpha_identity() +
  guides(
    colour = guide_legend(override.aes = list(alpha = 1))
  ) +

  labs(
    x = "Fold change relative to control",
    y = NULL
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    legend.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold", colour = "grey30"),
    legend.text  = element_text(size = 11, colour = "grey30"),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "grey100"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold", colour = "grey30"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold", colour = "grey30"),
    panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA, colour = "grey30"),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(colour = "grey95", size = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(colour = "grey95", size = 0.2)
  )

fa_snails_relative_changes

# save the plot
ggsave(
  "output/fa_snails_relative_changes.png",
  plot = fa_snails_relative_changes,
  width = 16.5,
  height = 12,
  units = "cm",
  dpi = 600
)

4) Total FA per snail

# total FA 
total_FA_snails <- bind_rows(total_SAFA_per_snail, total_MUFA_per_snail, total_PUFA_per_snail)

total_FA_per_snail <- total_FA_snails %>%
  dplyr::group_by(replicate, treatment) %>%
  dplyr::summarize(sum_FA = sum(sum_SAFA, sum_MUFA, sum_PUFA, na.rm = TRUE))
`summarise()` has grouped output by 'replicate'. You can override using the
`.groups` argument.
print(total_FA_per_snail)
# A tibble: 39 × 3
# Groups:   replicate [39]
   replicate treatment sum_FA
   <chr>     <chr>      <dbl>
 1 S_1       NC          594.
 2 S_10      NC          269.
 3 S_11      Pro         425.
 4 S_12      Pro         266.
 5 S_13      Pro         225.
 6 S_14      Pro         316.
 7 S_15      Pro         186.
 8 S_16      Pro         187.
 9 S_17      Pro         345.
10 S_18      Pro         368.
# ℹ 29 more rows
# Calculate mean, se and 95% CI per treatment
mean_FA_sa <- total_FA_per_snail %>%
  dplyr::group_by(treatment) %>%
  dplyr::summarize(
    mean_FA = mean(sum_FA, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = sum(!is.na(sum_FA)),
    se = sd(sum_FA, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n),
    t_crit = qt(0.975, df = n - 1),
    ci_lower = mean_FA - t_crit * se,
    ci_upper = mean_FA + t_crit * se,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(treatment, mean_FA, ci_lower, ci_upper, se)

# Extract mean of control group for percental difference
control_mean_FA_sa <- mean_FA_sa %>%
  filter(treatment == "NC") %>%
  pull(mean_FA)

# Calculate percental difference from control
mean_FA_sa <- mean_FA_sa %>%
  mutate(percent_diff_from_NC = 100 * (mean_FA - control_mean_FA_sa) / control_mean_FA_sa)

print(mean_FA_sa)
# A tibble: 4 × 6
  treatment mean_FA ci_lower ci_upper    se percent_diff_from_NC
  <chr>       <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> <dbl>                <dbl>
1 Cip          430.     389.     470.  17.7                 3.91
2 Mix          269.     208.     329.  26.2               -34.9 
3 NC           413.     353.     474.  26.6                 0   
4 Pro          310.     245.     376.  29.1               -24.9 
# linear model with total FA per snail
# treatment as factor
total_FA_per_snail$treatment <- as.factor(total_FA_per_snail$treatment)

model_FA_snails <- lm(sum_FA ~ treatment, data = total_FA_per_snail)
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
plot(model_FA_snails) # normality acceptable

qreference(residuals(model_FA_snails)) 

drop1(model_FA_snails, test = 'F')
Single term deletions

Model:
sum_FA ~ treatment
          Df Sum of Sq    RSS    AIC F value    Pr(>F)    
<none>                 217883 344.50                      
treatment  3    175881 393764 361.58  9.4177 0.0001057 ***
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
# treatment highly significant 

# conduct Tukey HSD-test for pairwise comparisons
FA_snails_tukey <- lsmeans(model_FA_snails, ~ treatment)
FA_snails_tukey
 treatment lsmean   SE df lower.CL upper.CL
 Cip          430 25.0 35      379      480
 Mix          269 26.3 35      216      322
 NC           413 25.0 35      363      464
 Pro          310 25.0 35      260      361

Confidence level used: 0.95 
FA_snails_tukey1 <- contrast(FA_snails_tukey, 'pairwise')
FA_snails_tukey
 treatment lsmean   SE df lower.CL upper.CL
 Cip          430 25.0 35      379      480
 Mix          269 26.3 35      216      322
 NC           413 25.0 35      363      464
 Pro          310 25.0 35      260      361

Confidence level used: 0.95 
FA_snails_summary <- summary(FA_snails_tukey1, infer = TRUE, adjust = 'mvt')
FA_snails_summary
 contrast  estimate   SE df lower.CL upper.CL t.ratio p.value
 Cip - Mix    160.6 36.3 35     62.9    258.3   4.430  0.0005
 Cip - NC      16.2 35.3 35    -78.9    111.2   0.458  0.9676
 Cip - Pro    119.1 35.3 35     24.1    214.2   3.376  0.0090
 Mix - NC    -144.4 36.3 35   -242.1    -46.8  -3.984  0.0017
 Mix - Pro    -41.5 36.3 35   -139.2     56.2  -1.144  0.6652
 NC - Pro     103.0 35.3 35      7.9    198.0   2.918  0.0295

Confidence level used: 0.95 
Conf-level adjustment: mvt method for 6 estimates 
P value adjustment: mvt method for 6 tests 
# Cip vs. Mix p = 0.0005
# Cip vs. Pro p = 0.0095
# Mix vs. Control p = 0.001
# Control vs. Pro p = 0.02

Look into interaction of Cip and Pro on total FA of snails:

# interaction effect model with presence / absence of single xenobiotics
total_FA_per_snail$Cip <- ifelse(total_FA_per_snail$treatment %in% c("Cip", "Mix"),
                                1, 0)

total_FA_per_snail$Cip <- factor(total_FA_per_snail$Cip, levels = c(0, 1))

total_FA_per_snail$Pro <- ifelse(total_FA_per_snail$treatment %in% c("Pro", "Mix"),
                                1, 0)

total_FA_per_snail$Pro <- factor(total_FA_per_snail$Pro, levels = c(0, 1))

# sanity check
table(total_FA_per_snail$treatment,
      total_FA_per_snail$Cip,
      total_FA_per_snail$Pro)
, ,  = 0

     
       0  1
  Cip  0 10
  Mix  0  0
  NC  10  0
  Pro  0  0

, ,  = 1

     
       0  1
  Cip  0  0
  Mix  0  9
  NC   0  0
  Pro 10  0
# interaction effect model
model_mix_FA_snails <- lm(sum_FA ~ Cip * Pro, data = total_FA_per_snail)
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
plot(model_mix_FA_snails) 

qreference(residuals(model_mix_FA_snails)) # normality acceptable

drop1(model_mix_FA_snails, test = 'F')
Single term deletions

Model:
sum_FA ~ Cip * Pro
        Df Sum of Sq    RSS    AIC F value Pr(>F)
<none>               217883 344.50               
Cip:Pro  1    8077.6 225961 343.92  1.2976 0.2624
# Cip:Pro interaction not significant

# look deeper into interaction:
# df.ref only control values
df.ref.FA_snails <- total_FA_per_snail |>
  dplyr::filter(Cip == 0, Pro == 0) 

# remove grouping
df.ref.FA_snails <- df.ref.FA_snails %>%
  dplyr::ungroup()

nd_FA_snails <- nd.generator(df.ref.FA_snails, stressor1 = "Cip", stressor2 = "Pro", diff = NULL)

#sanity check  
table(nd_FA_snails$dataset, nd_FA_snails$Cip)
     
       0  1
  C   10  0
  S1   0 10
  S12  0 10
  S2  10  0
table(nd_FA_snails$dataset, nd_FA_snails$Pro)
     
       0  1
  C   10  0
  S1  10  0
  S12  0 10
  S2   0 10
#check for AD, MU (all values >0) and OD
hyp_all_FA_snails <- predictions(
  model_mix_FA_snails,
  newdata = nd_FA_snails,
  hypothesis = function(x) {
    hypothesis.function(
      x,
      stressor1 = "Cip",
      stressor2 = "Pro",
      null = "all",        # <- AD, MU, DO together
      strategy = "average"
    )
  }
) |>
  inferences(method = "simulation")

hyp_all_FA_snails

 Term Estimate    2.5 % 97.5 %
   ad  -57.626 -154.947 41.527
   do  -41.471 -107.844 30.274
   mu   -0.182   -0.479  0.117

Type: response
hyp_all_FA_snails$term <- dplyr::recode(hyp_all_FA_snails$term,
                                    "ad" = "AD",
                                    "do" = "DO",
                                    "mu" = "MU")

ggplot(hyp_all_FA_snails, aes(x = term, y = estimate)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high), width = 0.2) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(x = "Null model", y = "Interaction estimate")

Post-estimation inference following Pimentel et al. (2025) indicated that the additive, dominance, and multiplicative null models could not be rejected, as the confidence intervals of the interaction estimates overlapped zero. This suggests that the combined effect of ciprofloxacin and propyzamide did not deviate significantly from expectations based on independent single-stressor effects.

7.2 Histogram FA snails

hist(FA_Snails_absolute $ fa_conc) 

skewness(FA_Snails_absolute $ fa_conc, na.rm = TRUE) 
[1] 2.738149
shapiro.test(FA_Snails_absolute $ fa_conc)

    Shapiro-Wilk normality test

data:  FA_Snails_absolute$fa_conc
W = 0.54871, p-value < 2.2e-16

Fatty acid concentration not normal distributed and right skewed.

7.3 NMDS FA snails

Change table to wide format:

# change table to wide format (sqrt for square root transformation in next step)
FA_Snails_absolute_wide_sqrt <-
  pivot_wider(FA_Snails_absolute,
              names_from = fa,
              values_from = fa_conc)

# square root transformation, to reduce discriminatory power of dominant FA groups
FA_Snails_absolute_wide_sqrt[, 3:36] <- sqrt(FA_Snails_absolute_wide_sqrt[, 3:36])

# Only keep columns/FAs with more than 5 non-zero values (fatty acids that have just been found in 5 replicates will be removed)
FA_Snails_absolute_wide_sqrt <-
  FA_Snails_absolute_wide_sqrt %>%
  dplyr::select(
    1:2,
    where(~sum(. !=0, na.rm = TRUE)>5)
  )

Perform metaMDS:

# Remove first and second column for metaMDS
FA_Snails_absolute_wide_sqrt <- FA_Snails_absolute_wide_sqrt[, -c(1, 2)]

# Rename (- C, - () ) fatty acids for later plot
FA_Snails_absolute_wide_sqrt <- FA_Snails_absolute_wide_sqrt %>%
  rename(
    `12:0`     = `C12:0`,   
    `13:0`     = `C13:0`,
    `14:0`     = `C14:0`,
    `15:0`     = `C15:0`,
    `16:0`     = `C16:0`,
    `16:1`     = `C16:1`,
    `18:0`     = `C18:0`,
    `18:1`     = `C18:1`,
    `18:2n-6c` = `C18:2(n-6c)`,
    `18:3n-6`  = `C18:3(n-6)`,
    `18:3n-3`  = `C18:3(n-3)`,
    `18:4n-3`  = `C18:4(n-3)`,
    `20:0`     = `C20:0`,
    `20:1`     = `C20:1`,
    `20:2n-6`  = `C20:2(n-6)`,
    `20:3n-6`  = `C20:3(n-6)`,
    `20:4n-6`  = `C20:4(n-6)`,
    `20:5n-3`  = `C20:5(n-3)`,
    `22:1`     = `C22:1`,
    `22:5n-3`  = `C22:5(n-3)`,
    `22:6n-3`  = `C22:6(n-3)`
)


NMDS_Snails_absolute <-
  metaMDS(
    FA_Snails_absolute_wide_sqrt,
    autotransform = FALSE,
    k = 2 ,
    distance = "bray",
    na.rm = TRUE
  )
Run 0 stress 0.1089254 
Run 1 stress 0.1268053 
Run 2 stress 0.1255721 
Run 3 stress 0.1089254 
... Procrustes: rmse 1.124124e-05  max resid 4.937968e-05 
... Similar to previous best
Run 4 stress 0.1108248 
Run 5 stress 0.1139017 
Run 6 stress 0.1132554 
Run 7 stress 0.1123584 
Run 8 stress 0.1130751 
Run 9 stress 0.1210822 
Run 10 stress 0.1089254 
... New best solution
... Procrustes: rmse 1.685648e-06  max resid 6.824643e-06 
... Similar to previous best
Run 11 stress 0.115053 
Run 12 stress 0.1204659 
Run 13 stress 0.1265231 
Run 14 stress 0.1255721 
Run 15 stress 0.1178435 
Run 16 stress 0.1157788 
Run 17 stress 0.1178435 
Run 18 stress 0.1187656 
Run 19 stress 0.1254017 
Run 20 stress 0.1178435 
*** Best solution repeated 1 times
NMDS_Snails_absolute$stress
[1] 0.1089254

Create fa vectors:

# separation in SAFA; MUFA & PUFA to build vectors in NMDS
# "sa" = snail absolute
FA_variables.SAFAs_sa <-
  envfit(
    NMDS_Snails_absolute,
    subset(
      FA_Snails_absolute_wide_sqrt,
      select = c(
        "12:0",
        "13:0",
        "14:0",
        "15:0",
        "16:0",
        "18:0",
        "20:0"
      )
    )
    ,
    permutations = 999,
    na.rm = TRUE
  )

FA_variables.MUFAs_sa <-
  envfit(
    NMDS_Snails_absolute,
    subset(
      FA_Snails_absolute_wide_sqrt,
      select = c(
        "16:1",
        "18:1",
        "20:1",
        "22:1"
      )
    )
    ,
    permutations = 999,
    na.rm = TRUE
  )

FA_variables.PUFAs_sa <-
  envfit(
    NMDS_Snails_absolute,
    subset(
      FA_Snails_absolute_wide_sqrt,
      select = c(
        "18:2n-6c",
        "18:3n-6",
        "18:3n-3",
        "18:4n-3",
        "20:2n-6",
        "20:3n-6",
        "20:4n-6",
        "20:5n-3",
        "22:5n-3",
        "22:6n-3"
      )
    )
    ,
    permutations = 999,
    na.rm = TRUE
  )

# Extract NMDS scores 
data.scores_snail_absolute = as.data.frame(scores(NMDS_Snails_absolute)$sites)

Treatments <-
  FA_Snails_absolute $ treatment[FA_Snails_absolute $ fa == "C16:0"]

# add treatment column to NMDS scores
data.scores_snail_absolute$Treatment = Treatments

# vectors for SAFA, MUFA and PUFA
SAFAs_coord_cont_sa = as.data.frame(scores(FA_variables.SAFAs_sa, "vectors")) * ordiArrowMul(FA_variables.SAFAs_sa)
MUFAs_coord_cont_sa = as.data.frame(scores(FA_variables.MUFAs_sa, "vectors")) * ordiArrowMul(FA_variables.MUFAs_sa)
PUFAs_coord_cont_sa = as.data.frame(scores(FA_variables.PUFAs_sa, "vectors")) * ordiArrowMul(FA_variables.PUFAs_sa)

Plot NMDS:

# For the plot: reorder treatment variable in data.scores_snail_absolute
data.scores_snail_absolute$Treatment <- factor(
  data.scores_snail_absolute$Treatment,
  levels = c("NC", "Pro", "Cip", "Mix"),
  labels  = c("Control", "Propyzamide", "Ciprofloxacin", "Mixture")
)

# Compute convex hull for each Treatment
polygon_data_snails <- data.scores_snail_absolute %>%
  group_by(Treatment) %>%
  summarise(hull_points = list(data.frame(
    NMDS1 = NMDS1[chull(NMDS1, NMDS2)],
    NMDS2 = NMDS2[chull(NMDS1, NMDS2)]
  ))) %>%
  unnest(hull_points)

# Function to rescale coordinates
rescale_vectors <- function(df, scale_factor = 1) {
  df$length <- sqrt(df$NMDS1^2 + df$NMDS2^2)
  max_length <- max(df$length)
  df$NMDS1 <- df$NMDS1 / max_length * scale_factor
  df$NMDS2 <- df$NMDS2 / max_length * scale_factor
  df$length <- NULL
  return(df)
}

# Apply to each group 
PUFAs_coord_cont_sa <- rescale_vectors(PUFAs_coord_cont_sa, 0.4)
MUFAs_coord_cont_sa <- rescale_vectors(MUFAs_coord_cont_sa, 0.4)
SAFAs_coord_cont_sa <- rescale_vectors(SAFAs_coord_cont_sa, 0.4)

# plot
absol_snail_with_vectors <- ggplot(data = data.scores_snail_absolute, aes(x = NMDS1, y = NMDS2)) +
    geom_polygon(
    data = polygon_data_snails,
    aes(x = NMDS1, y = NMDS2, group = Treatment, fill = Treatment),
    alpha = 0.2,
    show.legend = FALSE
    ) +
  geom_point(
    aes(shape = Treatment, color = Treatment),
    size = 5,
    alpha = 1,
    show.legend = TRUE
  ) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("Control" = 16, "Propyzamide" = 17, "Ciprofloxacin" = 18, "Mixture" = 15)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Control" = "#009E73", "Propyzamide" = "#E69F00", "Ciprofloxacin" = "#56B4E9", "Mixture" = "#999999")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Control" = "#009E73", "Propyzamide" = "#E69F00", "Ciprofloxacin" = "#56B4E9", "Mixture" = "#999999")) + 
  geom_segment(aes(x = 0, y = 0, xend = NMDS1, yend = NMDS2), 
               data = PUFAs_coord_cont_sa, linewidth = 0.5, alpha = 0.2, colour = "black") +
  geom_segment(aes(x = 0, y = 0, xend = NMDS1, yend = NMDS2), 
               data = MUFAs_coord_cont_sa, linewidth = 0.5, alpha = 0.2, colour = "black") +
  geom_segment(aes(x = 0, y = 0, xend = NMDS1, yend = NMDS2), 
               data = SAFAs_coord_cont_sa, linewidth = 0.5, alpha = 0.2, colour = "black") +
  geom_text_repel(
    data = PUFAs_coord_cont_sa, 
    aes(x = NMDS1, y = NMDS2, label = row.names(PUFAs_coord_cont_sa)), 
    colour = "black",
    size = 2.5) +
  geom_text_repel(
    data = MUFAs_coord_cont_sa, 
    aes(x = NMDS1, y = NMDS2, label = row.names(MUFAs_coord_cont_sa)), 
    colour = "#996600",
    size = 2.5) +
  geom_text_repel(
    data = SAFAs_coord_cont_sa, 
    aes(x = NMDS1, y = NMDS2, label = row.names(SAFAs_coord_cont_sa)), 
    colour = "#0000FF",
    size = 2.5) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(
      size = 13,
      face = "bold",
      colour = "grey30"
    ),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA, colour = "grey30"),
    legend.key = element_blank(),
    legend.title = element_text(
      size = 11,
      face = "bold",
      colour = "grey30"
    ),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10, colour = "grey30"),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(colour = "grey95", size = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(colour = "grey95", size = 0.2)
  ) 

absol_snail_with_vectors

# position of FA names were manually adjusted for publication in vector graphic in the software Inkscape

Save the plot as .png:

ggsave(
  "output/NMDS_snails_absolute_vectors.png",
  width = 16.5,
  height = 12,
  units = "cm",
  dpi = 600
)

Save the plot as vector graphic (minor adaptions for better readability with Inkscape):

ggsave(
  "output/NMDS_snails_absolute_vectors.svg",
  device = "svg",
  width = 16.5,
  height = 16,
  units = "cm",
  dpi = 600
)

Now plot NMDS without fatty acid vectors:

# plot
absol_snail <- ggplot(data = data.scores_snail_absolute, aes(x = NMDS1, y = NMDS2)) +
    geom_polygon(
    data = polygon_data_snails,
    aes(x = NMDS1, y = NMDS2, group = Treatment, fill = Treatment),
    alpha = 0.2,
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_point(
    aes(shape = Treatment, color = Treatment),
    size = 5,
    alpha = 1,
    show.legend = TRUE
  ) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("Control" = 16, "Propyzamide" = 17, "Ciprofloxacin" = 18, "Mixture" = 15)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Control" = "#009E73", "Propyzamide" = "#E69F00", "Ciprofloxacin" = "#56B4E9", "Mixture" = "#999999")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Control" = "#009E73", "Propyzamide" = "#E69F00", "Ciprofloxacin" = "#56B4E9", "Mixture" = "#999999")) +  
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Control" = "#009E73", "Propyzamide" = "#E69F00", "Ciprofloxacin" = "#56B4E9", "Mixture" = "#999999")) + 
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(
      size = 11,
      face = "bold",
      colour = "grey30"
    ),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA, colour = "grey30"),
    legend.key = element_blank(),
    legend.title = element_text(
      size = 11,
      face = "bold",
      colour = "grey30"
    ),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10, colour = "grey30"),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(colour = "grey95", size = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(colour = "grey95", size = 0.2)
  ) 
Scale for colour is already present.
Adding another scale for colour, which will replace the existing scale.
absol_snail

Save the plot as .png:

ggsave(
  "output/NMDS_snails_absolute.png",
  width = 16.5,
  height = 12,
  units = "cm",
  dpi = 600
)

Save the plot as vector graphic:

ggsave(
  "output/NMDS_snails_absolute.svg",
  device = "svg",
  width = 16.5,
  height = 16,
  units = "cm",
  dpi = 600
)

7.4 PERMANOVA FA snails

Create distance matrix with fatty acids:

FA_Snails_dist <- vegdist(FA_Snails_absolute_wide_sqrt, method = 'bray')

Separate explanatory variables from fatty acid information:

# change initial table to wide format again
FA_Snails_absolute_wide_permanova <-
  pivot_wider(FA_Snails_absolute,
              names_from = fa,
              values_from = fa_conc)

# square root transformation again, to reduce discriminatory power of dominant fa groups
FA_Snails_absolute_wide_permanova[, 3:28] <- sqrt(FA_Snails_absolute_wide_permanova[, 3:28])

# Only keep columns/FAs with more than 5 non-zero values (fatty acids that have just been found in 5 replicates will be removed)
FA_Snails_absolute_wide_permanova <-
  FA_Snails_absolute_wide_permanova %>%
  dplyr::select(
    1:2,
    where(~sum(. !=0, na.rm = TRUE)>5)
  )

# delete fa columns
FA_Snails_absolute_wide_permanova <- FA_Snails_absolute_wide_permanova[, c(1, 2)]
FA_Snails_absolute_wide_permanova $ treatment <- as.factor(FA_Snails_absolute_wide_permanova $ treatment)

Perform PERMANOVA:

perm_FA_Snails <- adonis2(FA_Snails_dist ~ treatment, data = FA_Snails_absolute_wide_permanova, permutations = 999)
print(perm_FA_Snails)
Permutation test for adonis under reduced model
Terms added sequentially (first to last)
Permutation: free
Number of permutations: 999

adonis2(formula = FA_Snails_dist ~ treatment, data = FA_Snails_absolute_wide_permanova, permutations = 999)
          Df SumOfSqs      R2      F Pr(>F)    
treatment  3  0.34282 0.50206 11.763  0.001 ***
Residual  35  0.34001 0.49794                  
Total     38  0.68283 1.00000                  
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

Pairwise comparisons (by: Martinez Arbizu, P. (2020). pairwiseAdonis: Pairwise multilevel comparison using adonis. R package version 0.4):

pairwise_results <- pairwise.adonis2(
  FA_Snails_dist ~ treatment,
  data = FA_Snails_absolute_wide_permanova,
  permutations = 999
)

# View the results
print(pairwise_results)
$parent_call
[1] "FA_Snails_dist ~ treatment , strata = Null , permutations 999"

$NC_vs_Pro
          Df SumOfSqs      R2      F Pr(>F)    
treatment  1  0.10971 0.35642 9.9684  0.001 ***
Residual  18  0.19811 0.64358                  
Total     19  0.30782 1.00000                  
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

          Df SumOfSqs      R2     F Pr(>F)    
treatment  1  0.18442 0.52422 18.73  0.001 ***
Residual  17  0.16738 0.47578                 
Total     18  0.35180 1.00000                 
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

$NC_vs_Cip
          Df SumOfSqs      R2      F Pr(>F)    
treatment  1 0.046091 0.32447 8.6456  0.001 ***
Residual  18 0.095962 0.67553                  
Total     19 0.142053 1.00000                  
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

$Pro_vs_Mix
          Df SumOfSqs      R2      F Pr(>F)
treatment  1 0.026685 0.09856 1.8588  0.122
Residual  17 0.244050 0.90144              
Total     18 0.270735 1.00000              

$Pro_vs_Cip
          Df SumOfSqs      R2      F Pr(>F)    
treatment  1  0.14011 0.44801 14.609  0.001 ***
Residual  18  0.17263 0.55199                  
Total     19  0.31274 1.00000                  
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

$Mix_vs_Cip
          Df SumOfSqs      R2      F Pr(>F)    
treatment  1  0.18109 0.56066 21.694  0.001 ***
Residual  17  0.14190 0.43934                  
Total     18  0.32299 1.00000                  
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

attr(,"class")
[1] "pwadstrata" "list"      
# Cip*Pro interaction?
# add presence / absence columns
FA_Snails_absolute_wide_permanova$Cip <- ifelse(FA_Snails_absolute_wide_permanova$treatment %in% c("Cip", "Mix"),
                                                 1,
                                                 0)

FA_Snails_absolute_wide_permanova$Cip <- 
  factor(FA_Snails_absolute_wide_permanova$Cip, levels = c(0, 1))

FA_Snails_absolute_wide_permanova$Pro <- ifelse(FA_Snails_absolute_wide_permanova$treatment %in% c("Pro", "Mix"),
                                                 1,
                                                 0)

FA_Snails_absolute_wide_permanova$Pro <- 
  factor(FA_Snails_absolute_wide_permanova$Pro, levels = c(0, 1))

# sanity check
table(FA_Snails_absolute_wide_permanova$treatment,
      FA_Snails_absolute_wide_permanova$Cip)
     
       0  1
  Cip  0 10
  Mix  0  9
  NC  10  0
  Pro 10  0
table(FA_Snails_absolute_wide_permanova$treatment,
      FA_Snails_absolute_wide_permanova$Pro)
     
       0  1
  Cip 10  0
  Mix  0  9
  NC  10  0
  Pro  0 10
# permanova
perm_FA_snails_Cip_Pro <- adonis2(FA_Snails_dist ~ Cip * Pro,
        data = FA_Snails_absolute_wide_permanova,
        permutations = 999)

print(perm_FA_snails_Cip_Pro)
Permutation test for adonis under reduced model
Terms added sequentially (first to last)
Permutation: free
Number of permutations: 999

adonis2(formula = FA_Snails_dist ~ Cip * Pro, data = FA_Snails_absolute_wide_permanova, permutations = 999)
         Df SumOfSqs      R2       F Pr(>F)    
Cip       1  0.05202 0.07618  5.3548  0.007 ** 
Pro       1  0.27117 0.39713 27.9140  0.001 ***
Cip:Pro   1  0.01963 0.02874  2.0202  0.093 .  
Residual 35  0.34001 0.49794                   
Total    38  0.68283 1.00000                   
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
# no interaction

All treatments except of Pro_vs_Mix significant differences. No Cip * Pro interaction.

7.5 SIMPER FA snails

# Perform SIMPER
simper_FA_Snails <-
  with(
    FA_Snails_absolute_wide_permanova,
    simper(FA_Snails_absolute_wide_sqrt, treatment,  permutations = 999)
  )

summary(simper_FA_Snails)

Contrast: NC_Pro

 - attr(*, "class")= chr "simper"
 - attr(*, "permutations")= int 999
 - attr(*, "control")=List of 12
  ..$ within     :List of 6
  .. ..$ type    : chr "free"
  .. ..$ constant: logi FALSE
  .. ..$ mirror  : logi FALSE
  .. ..$ ncol    : NULL
  .. ..$ nrow    : NULL
  .. ..$ call    : language Within()
  .. ..- attr(*, "class")= chr "Within"
  ..$ plots      :List of 7
  .. ..$ strata    : NULL
  .. ..$ type      : chr "none"
  .. ..$ mirror    : logi FALSE
  .. ..$ ncol      : NULL
  .. ..$ nrow      : NULL
  .. ..$ plots.name: chr "NULL"
  .. ..$ call      : language Plots()
  .. ..- attr(*, "class")= chr "Plots"
  ..$ blocks     : NULL
  ..$ nperm      : num 999
  ..$ complete   : logi FALSE
  ..$ maxperm    : num 9999
  ..$ minperm    : num 5040
  ..$ all.perms  : NULL
  ..$ make       : logi TRUE
  ..$ observed   : logi FALSE
  ..$ blocks.name: chr "NULL"
  ..$ call       : language how(nperm = 999)
  ..- attr(*, "class")= chr "how"
# save tsv tables of FAs which contribute > 70% to differences
simper_sum_snails <- summary(simper_FA_Snails)

out_dir <- "output"
dir.create(out_dir, showWarnings = FALSE)

# Safe filename helper
safe_name <- function(x) gsub("[^A-Za-z0-9_]+", "_", x)

iwalk(simper_sum_snails, function(x, nm) {
  if (is.null(x)) return(invisible(NULL))

  # SIMPER summary as a data.frame:
  # columns: average, sd, ratio, ava, avb, cumsum, p
  # rownames are the fatty acid IDs
  df <- as.data.frame(x)

  # keep rows up to the first that reaches/exceeds 70%
  idx <- which(df$cumsum >= 0.70)[1]
  if (is.na(idx)) idx <- nrow(df)
  out <- df[seq_len(idx), , drop = FALSE]

  # just keep 3 decimels
  out_write <- out
  num_cols <- vapply(out_write, is.numeric, logical(1))
  out_write[, num_cols] <- lapply(out_write[, num_cols, drop = FALSE],
                                  function(col) formatC(col, format = "f", digits = 3))

  file <- file.path(out_dir, paste0(safe_name(nm), "_top70_snails.tsv"))
  # write row names so the TSV mirrors the SIMPER structure
  write.table(out_write, file, sep = "\t", quote = FALSE, col.names = NA)
})

# Combine results into a data frame
simper_results_snails <- do.call(rbind, lapply(simper_FA_Snails, function(x) {
  if (!is.null(x$p)) {
    data.frame(
      fatty_acid = names(x$p),  
      p = as.numeric(x$p),      
      average = as.numeric(x$average),  
      sd = as.numeric(x$sd),
      ratio = as.numeric(x$ratio),
      cusum = as.numeric(x$cusum),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  } else {
    NULL  
  }
}))

# Show only significant results
simper_results_snails %>%
  dplyr::filter(p <= 0.05) %>%
  dplyr::select(fatty_acid, p, average, sd, ratio, cusum)  
           fatty_acid     p     average          sd    ratio     cusum

# How often do they occur
simper_results_snails %>%
  dplyr::filter(p <= 0.05) %>%
  dplyr::count(fatty_acid) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(desc(n)) %>%
  print()
   fatty_acid n
1        14:0 4
2        15:0 4
3        12:0 2

15    20:5n-3 1

8 Temperature and light conditions

8.1 Biofilm colonisation and contamination

# insert data
colonisation_temp_light <-
  read.table(file.path("data\\colonisation_temp_light.txt"),
             header = TRUE, sep = "\t")

View(colonisation_temp_light)

# average temperature
mean(colonisation_temp_light $ temperature_colonisation)
[1] 18.28751
# 18.28751 °C

# mean light intensity
# filter only daytime
average_light_intensity_colonisation <- colonisation_temp_light %>%
  dplyr::filter(light_intensity_colonisation > 0) %>%
  dplyr::summarize(average_light_intensity_colonisation = mean(light_intensity_colonisation))

print(average_light_intensity_colonisation)
  average_light_intensity_colonisation
1                             14019.26
# 14019.26 lux

# temperature plot
# add a small amount of random noise to the y-values
colonisation_temp_light $ temperature_colonisation <- jitter(colonisation_temp_light $ temperature_colonisation)

t2 <- ggplot(
  data = colonisation_temp_light,
  mapping = aes( x = day, y = temperature_colonisation, color = time_of_day)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +  # Individual measurements with transparency
  geom_smooth() +
  labs(
    x = "Day", y = "Temperature (°C)"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 77, by = 5)) +
  scale_color_continuous(
    name = "Time of Day",  
    breaks = c(0, 5, 10, 15, 20),
    labels = c("08:00 pm", "03:00 pm", "10:00 am", "05:00 am", "0:00 am"),
    limits = c(0, 22),
  )

t2 
`geom_smooth()` using method = 'gam' and formula = 'y ~ s(x, bs = "cs")'

# save the plot
ggsave(
  "output/temperature_colonisation.png",
  width = 16.5,
  height = 12,
  units = "cm",
  dpi = 600
)
`geom_smooth()` using method = 'gam' and formula = 'y ~ s(x, bs = "cs")'
# light intensity plot
# Filter the data to include only rows where light_intensity > 0
filtered_data2 <- colonisation_temp_light %>% 
  filter(light_intensity_colonisation > 0)

# add a small amount of random noise to the y-values
filtered_data2 $ light_intensity_colonisation <- jitter(filtered_data2 $ light_intensity_colonisation)

l2 <- ggplot(
  data = filtered_data2,
  mapping = aes( x = day, y = light_intensity_colonisation)) +
  geom_point(size = 1) +  # Individual measurements with transparency
  geom_smooth(color = "#E69F00", linewidth = 1) +
  labs(
    x = "Day", y = "Light intensity (lux)"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 77, by = 5)) 

l2 
`geom_smooth()` using method = 'gam' and formula = 'y ~ s(x, bs = "cs")'

# save the plot
ggsave(
  "output/light_intensity_colonisation.png",
  width = 16.5,
  height = 12,
  units = "cm",
  dpi = 600
)
`geom_smooth()` using method = 'gam' and formula = 'y ~ s(x, bs = "cs")'

8.2 Feeding assay

# insert data
feeding_assay_temp_light <-
  read.table(file.path("data\\feeding_assay_temp_light.txt"),
             header = TRUE, 
             sep = "\t"
  )
             
View(feeding_assay_temp_light)

# average temperature
mean(feeding_assay_temp_light $ temperature)
[1] 13.27927
# 13.27927 °C

# mean light intensity
# filter only daytime
average_light_intensity_feeding <- feeding_assay_temp_light %>%
  dplyr::filter(light_intensity > 0) %>%
  dplyr::summarize(average_light_intensity_feeding = mean(light_intensity))

print(average_light_intensity_feeding)
  average_light_intensity_feeding
1                        330.3552
# 330.3552 lux

# temperature plot
# add a small amount of random noise to the y-values
feeding_assay_temp_light $ temperature <- jitter(feeding_assay_temp_light $ temperature)

t1 <- ggplot(
  data = feeding_assay_temp_light,
  mapping = aes( x = day, y = temperature, color = time_of_day)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +  # Individual measurements with transparency
  geom_smooth() +
  labs(
    x = "Day", y = "Temperature (°C)"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:21) +
  scale_color_continuous(
    name = "Time of Day",  
    breaks = c(0, 5, 10, 15, 20),
    labels = c("08:00 pm", "03:00 pm", "10:00 am", "05:00 am", "0:00 am"),
    limits = c(0, 22),
  )

t1 
`geom_smooth()` using method = 'loess' and formula = 'y ~ x'

# save the plot
ggsave(
  "output/temperature_feeding_assay.png",
  width = 16.5,
  height = 12,
  units = "cm",
  dpi = 600
)
`geom_smooth()` using method = 'loess' and formula = 'y ~ x'
# light intensity plot
# Filter the data to include only rows where light_intensity > 0
filtered_data1 <- feeding_assay_temp_light %>% 
  filter(light_intensity > 0)

# add a small amount of random noise to the y-values
filtered_data1 $ light_intensity <- jitter(filtered_data1 $ light_intensity)

l1 <- ggplot(
  data = filtered_data1,
  mapping = aes( x = day, y = light_intensity)) +
  geom_point(size = 1) +  # Individual measurements with transparency
  geom_smooth(color = "#E69F00", linewidth = 1) +
  labs(
    x = "Day", y = "Light intensity (lux)"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:21) 

l1 
`geom_smooth()` using method = 'loess' and formula = 'y ~ x'

# save the plot
ggsave(
  "output/light_intensity_feeding_assay.png",
  width = 16.5,
  height = 12,
  units = "cm",
  dpi = 600
)
`geom_smooth()` using method = 'loess' and formula = 'y ~ x'

9 Chemical analyses

# insert data
chemical_analyses <-
  read.table(file.path("data\\chemical_analyses.txt"),
             header = TRUE, sep = "\t")

View(chemical_analyses)

# LOQ to NA
chemical_analyses[chemical_analyses == "<LOQ"] <- NA
# concentration values as numeric
chemical_analyses$concentration_ug_L <- as.numeric(
  ifelse(chemical_analyses$concentration_ug_L == "<LOQ", NA, chemical_analyses$concentration_ug_L)
)

# calculate means, se, 95% CI
chemical_means <- chemical_analyses %>%
  filter(!is.na(concentration_ug_L)) %>% 
  dplyr::mutate(percent_deviation = 100 * (concentration_ug_L - nominal_conc) / nominal_conc) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(treatment, mixture) %>%
  dplyr::summarize(
    mean_concentration = mean(concentration_ug_L, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = sum(!is.na(concentration_ug_L)),
    se = sd(concentration_ug_L, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n),
    t_crit = qt(0.975, df = n - 1),
    ci_lower = mean_concentration - t_crit * se,
    ci_upper = mean_concentration + t_crit * se,
    mean_percent_deviation = mean(percent_deviation, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_percent_deviation = sd(percent_deviation, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(sum(!is.na(percent_deviation))),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(treatment, mixture, mean_concentration, ci_lower, ci_upper, se, mean_percent_deviation, se_percent_deviation)

print(chemical_means)
# A tibble: 6 × 8
  treatment                 mixture mean_concentration ci_lower ci_upper      se
  <chr>                     <chr>                <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>   <dbl>
1 ciprofloxacin             no                   73.7     39.0    108.   1.25e+1
2 ciprofloxacin             yes                  56.6     46.4     66.7  3.18e+0
3 propyzamide               no                    6.85     5.92     7.78 3.36e-1
4 propyzamide               yes                   6.80     5.80     7.79 3.12e-1
5 stock_solution_ciproflox… no                61770.   49710.   73831.   2.80e+3
6 stock_solution_propyzami… no                 6521.    4867.    8175.   3.84e+2
# ℹ 2 more variables: mean_percent_deviation <dbl>, se_percent_deviation <dbl>
